### 2025021742004569_Unable%20to%20Connect%20to%20HPC%20Clusters%20and%20JupyterHub.md
# Ticket 2025021742004569

 # HPC Support Ticket: Unable to Connect to HPC Clusters and JupyterHub

## Keywords
- HPC account
- Clusters
- Alex server
- JupyterHub
- Access issues
- Error screenshots

## Problem Description
- User recently received an HPC account but is facing issues connecting to the clusters, particularly the Alex server.
- User is also unable to access JupyterHub.
- Error screenshots were attached for reference.

## Root Cause
- Users do not get access to the Alex server by default.
- Initial access issues with Tinygpu and JupyterHub.

## Solution
- **Tinygpu and JupyterHub Access:**
  - HPC Admins granted access to Tinygpu and JupyterHub.
- **Alex Server Access:**
  - Users need to fill out a form to request access to the Alex server.
  - Form link: [https://hpc.fau.de/tier3-access-to-alex/](https://hpc.fau.de/tier3-access-to-alex/)

## General Learnings
- New users may not have default access to all HPC resources.
- Specific access requests may require additional forms or permissions.
- Initial access issues can often be resolved by HPC Admins granting the necessary permissions.

## Next Steps for Similar Issues
- Verify if the user has the necessary permissions for the specific resource.
- Provide the appropriate form or contact information for requesting additional access.
- Ensure that the user is aware of the default access levels and the process for requesting additional resources.
---

### 2022012142001811_Registering%20for%20HPC.md
# Ticket 2022012142001811

 # HPC Support Ticket: Registering for HPC

## Keywords
- HPC Registration
- New User
- HPC-Kennung
- IdM-Kennung
- HPC-Cafe
- Password Setup

## Summary
A new PhD student requests registration for HPC resources. The HPC Admin processes the request and provides instructions for setting up the account.

## Root Cause
- User needs to register for HPC resources.

## Solution
1. **Account Creation**:
   - HPC Admin creates an HPC-Kennung (e.g., `mpt2022h`) for the user.
   - User is instructed to log in with their IdM-Kennung (e.g., `aq06uhaq`) at [www.idm.fau.de](http://www.idm.fau.de) to set a password for the new HPC-Kennung.

2. **Password Setup**:
   - User should set a password under "Dienstleistungen" on the IdM portal.
   - Password changes may take a few hours to be recognized on HPC systems.

3. **Service Initialization**:
   - It may take up to 2 days for services to be initially set up on HPC servers.

4. **User Support**:
   - New users are encouraged to attend the HPC-Cafe, held every second Tuesday of the month at 16:00 in Room 2.049, RRZE, Martensstr. 1.
   - Due to COVID-19, the HPC-Cafe may be held online. Details are available on the [HPC-Cafe website](https://hpc.fau.de/services/hpc-cafe/).

## General Learnings
- New users should follow the instructions provided by HPC Admins for account setup.
- Password changes and service initialization may take some time.
- The HPC-Cafe is a valuable resource for new users to get support and information.

## Notes
- The ticket was initially moved due to a new request and later closed after the user was successfully registered.
- The user's department and specific software needs were noted for future reference.
---

### 2023053042000296_Umstellung%20von%20gwgk%20das%20rein%20elektronische%20HPC-Portal.md
# Ticket 2023053042000296

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket Conversation: Transition to Electronic HPC Portal

## Keywords
- HPC Portal
- SSH Keys
- IdM System
- Tier3-FAU-Grundversorgung
- Single Sign-on (SSO)
- Exportkontrolle
- ClusterCockpit
- Jupyterhub
- Projektkategorien

## Summary
The HPC support team is transitioning HPC accounts from paper-based applications and the IdM portal to a new electronic HPC portal. This transition involves several steps and deadlines, with significant changes in how users access HPC systems.

## Key Points to Learn

### Transition Steps
1. **Import of Existing Accounts**: Existing HPC accounts have been imported into the HPC portal. Users can now upload SSH public keys through the portal.
2. **PI and Manager Roles**: Users who have logged into the HPC portal will be designated as PI (Principal Investigator) and Manager of their group. They will receive an automated email once this role is assigned.
3. **SSH Key Requirement**: Starting June 12, users who have logged into the HPC portal will need to use SSH keys for login. Passwords will no longer be set for these accounts.
4. **Ablaufdatum**: The HPC expiration date for all existing accounts will be set to the end of June in the IdM system. Users will receive an automated email about this change.
5. **Final Deadline**: By July 1, all users must use SSH keys for login, regardless of whether they have logged into the HPC portal before.

### Benefits of the HPC Portal
- **Elimination of Paper-based Applications**: New HPC accounts can be created through invitations sent via the HPC portal.
- **Self-Management**: PI/Managers can manage the lifecycle of HPC accounts, including extending or suspending accounts.
- **Security**: The use of SSH keys increases security by requiring both a passphrase and the key itself.
- **Monitoring**: PI/Managers can view the compute time usage of their group and individual accounts.
- **Additional Services**: Access to ClusterCockpit and Jupyterhub will be managed through the HPC portal.

### User Concerns and Solutions
- **SSH Key Upload**: Users can upload SSH keys at any time, and there is no specific deadline. However, by June 30, all users must have uploaded their SSH keys to continue accessing HPC systems.

## Root Cause and Solution
- **Root Cause**: The transition to the HPC portal requires users to adopt SSH keys for login, which some users may find challenging.
- **Solution**: Users can upload SSH keys at any time through the HPC portal. Detailed instructions and FAQs are available to assist with this process.

## Additional Notes
- **Projektkategorien**: The HPC portal supports three project categories: Tier3-Grundversorgung, Studentische Abschlussarbeiten, and Projektpartner. These categories are currently used for statistical purposes but may affect re-validation frequency in the future.
- **Exportkontrolle**: PI/Managers are responsible for ensuring that HPC accounts comply with export control regulations.

## Conclusion
The transition to the electronic HPC portal aims to streamline account management and enhance security. Users should familiarize themselves with the new portal and ensure they have uploaded their SSH keys by the specified deadlines.
```
---

### 2025021042003251_HPC-Portal%20Tier3%20request%20-%20mfdr.md
# Ticket 2025021042003251

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC-Portal
- Tier3 Project
- Radiologie
- Uniklinik Erlangen
- Exportkontrolle
- Einrichtungsleiter
- HPC-Portal Anmeldung
- FAU.org-Nummer
- RRZE-Präfix
- mfdr

## General Learnings
- The process requires the head of the institution (Einrichtungsleiter) to log into the HPC-Portal and accept the export control regulations.
- The head can then delegate the day-to-day management (Verwaltung der Accounts) to another person via a formless email.
- The export control declaration cannot be delegated.
- The HPC-Portal process is designed to be self-explanatory, with additional documentation available at [HPC-Portal Anleitung](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/).

## Root Cause of the Problem
- The user was unsure if there was already a responsible PI for the Radiologie department who could invite people to the HPC-Portal.
- The user requested that their PI (Sebastian Bickelhaupt) be given the necessary rights for a Tier3 project if no responsible PI existed.

## Solution
- The HPC Admin confirmed that no Tier3 project for Radiologie existed.
- The user was advised to have the head of the institution (Prof. Uder) log into the HPC-Portal to accept the export control regulations.
- After logging in, the head can delegate the day-to-day management to another person via email.
- The user was provided with a link to the HPC-Portal documentation for further assistance.

## Additional Notes
- The user was not aware of the FAU.org-Nummer but confirmed the RRZE-Präfix (mfdr) for the diagnostische Radiologie.
- The HPC Admin suggested that the steps in the HPC-Portal should be self-explanatory, but additional documentation is available if needed.
---

### 2025011642000521_Workspace%20Recovery%20-%20dringend.md
# Ticket 2025011642000521

 # Workspace Recovery - dringend

## Problem Description
- **Root Cause**: Workspace expiration.
- **Symptom**: User receives the message "Der Arbeitsbereich ist nicht mehr vorhanden" when trying to access the workspace.

## Solution
- **Recovery Window**: Workspaces can be recovered up to 10 days after expiration.
- **List Expired Workspaces**: Use the command `ws_restore -l` to list available expired workspaces.
- **Restore Workspace**: Use the command `ws_restore -n [name of the expired workspace] -t [new workspace]` to restore the workspace.

## Keywords
- Workspace recovery
- Expired workspace
- `ws_restore` command
- HPC support
- Workspace management

## General Learnings
- Expired workspaces can be recovered within a specific time frame.
- Commands are available to list and restore expired workspaces.
- Quick recovery of workspaces is crucial for resuming computational jobs.

## Roles Involved
- **HPC Admins**: Provide guidance on workspace recovery.
- **2nd Level Support Team**: Assist with technical issues and workspace management.
- **Head of the Datacenter**: Oversees datacenter operations.
- **Training and Support Group Leader**: Manages training and support activities.
- **NHR Rechenzeit Support**: Handles computing time support and grant applications.
- **Software and Tools Developer**: Develops and maintains software tools for HPC.
---

### 42073332_Inkonsistenz%20%24HOME%20und%20%24GROUP%20bei%20mpo314.md
# Ticket 42073332

 # HPC Support Ticket: Inconsistency in $HOME and $GROUP for User

## Keywords
- Group change
- Inconsistent group membership
- Home directory permissions
- Symlink solution

## Problem Description
- User noticed a group change from `mpo3` to `mpob` via LXFS-Mail.
- The change was not fully implemented, leading to inconsistencies in group membership and home directory permissions.
- `$HOME` and other directories had different group settings.
- The new group directory `/home/vault/mpob/mpo314` had not been created.

## Root Cause
- Incomplete update of group membership and home directory permissions after a group change.

## Solution
- HPC Admin created a symlink under `/home/{hpc,vault}/mpob/mpo314` to maintain the usual `/$GROUP/$USER` access schema.
- Changing the group for an existing directory or moving the home directory was not recommended.

## General Learnings
- Group changes can lead to inconsistencies in user directories and permissions.
- Symlinks can be used to maintain access schemas without altering existing directories.
- Ensure that all relevant directories are updated when changing user groups.
---

### 2024062542003754_access%20to%20alex.md
# Ticket 2024062542003754

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Access to Alex

## Keywords
- Access issues
- Alex cluster
- Default permissions
- Access request form

## Problem Description
- User unable to connect to Alex cluster.
- Connection to other clusters is successful.
- Connection to Alex is closed immediately.

## Root Cause
- FAU HPC accounts do not have access to Alex by default.

## Solution
- If TinyGPU is insufficient, users need to request access to Alex by filling out the form: [Tier3 Access to Alex](https://hpc.fau.de/tier3-access-to-alex/).

## General Learnings
- Default permissions for FAU HPC accounts do not include access to Alex.
- Users need to explicitly request access to Alex if their computational needs exceed TinyGPU capabilities.
- The access request process involves filling out a specific form.
```
---

 ```markdown
## General Learnings
- Accounts can be temporarily reactivated to allow users to transfer data.
- There may be a delay in the propagation of account reactivation information across systems.
```
---

### 2024093042002838_Re%3A%20Your%20account%20%22iwi5141h%22%20for%20project%20%22Studentische%20Abschlu%C3%83%C2%9Farbei.md
# Ticket 2024093042002838

 # HPC Support Ticket: Account Expiration Issue

## Keywords
- Account expiration
- Access issue
- Extension request
- Project manager
- PI (Principal Investigator)

## Summary
A user reported being unable to access their HPC account and inquired if it had already expired. The HPC Admin confirmed that the account had indeed expired.

## Root Cause
The user's HPC account had reached its expiration date, preventing access to the system.

## Solution
- **User Action**: The user should request an extension from their Principal Investigator (PI) or project managers if the project runtime allows for it.
- **Admin Action**: Confirm the account expiration and advise the user on the next steps for extension.

## General Learnings
- **Account Management**: Users should be aware of their account expiration dates and plan for extensions if needed.
- **Communication**: Clear communication between users, PIs, and project managers is essential for managing account lifecycles.
- **Support Process**: Users should be directed to the appropriate documentation and support channels for resolving account-related issues.

## References
- [HPC Portal Documentation](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/)
- Support Email: `hpc-support@fau.de`

## Next Steps
- Users should follow up with their PIs or project managers for account extensions.
- HPC Admins should monitor account expirations and provide timely notifications to users.
---

### 42110744_HPC-Account.md
# Ticket 42110744

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket: HPC-Account

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Home Directory
- User Setup
- Password Setup
- Central User Management

## Summary
- **User Issue**: User noticed that they did not have a personal directory under `capn` on the HPC servers.
- **Root Cause**: Incorrect home path was entered in the central user management system.
- **Solution**: Corrected the home path in the central user management system. The home directory will be automatically created within the next few hours.

## Detailed Conversation
1. **HPC Admin**: Informed the user that their HPC account (`mppi32`) has been created. The user was instructed to set a password for the new account via the provided link. The admin also mentioned that it might take up to 2 days for the services to be fully set up on the HPC servers.
2. **User**: Noticed that they did not have a personal directory under `capn` on the HPC servers and requested one to be set up.
3. **HPC Admin**: Identified that an incorrect home path was entered in the central user management system. The admin corrected the path and informed the user that their home directory will be automatically created within the next few hours.
4. **User**: Acknowledged the solution.
5. **HPC Admin**: Marked the ticket as resolved.

## Lessons Learned
- Ensure that the home path is correctly set up in the central user management system to avoid issues with user directories.
- Users should be informed about the potential delay in service setup and the steps to set up their passwords.
- Quick resolution of incorrect configurations can prevent further user issues.
```
---

### 2019120242001252_HPC%20Account%20Wiederherstellen.md
# Ticket 2019120242001252

 # HPC Account Expiration and Restoration

## Keywords
- HPC Account Expiration
- Account Restoration
- IdM-Kennung
- RRZE Services
- FAU Students
- FAU Employees
- Guests and Others

## Summary
A user's HPC account expired due to misunderstanding the service expiration notification. The user requested account restoration, including data recovery.

## Root Cause
- The user received an email notification about expiring RRZE services but did not understand the implications.
- The user assumed the notification was related to their FAU contract expiration and did not take necessary actions.

## Solution
- The user needs to submit an application to restore the HPC account.
- The HPC Admins need to verify if the user's data can be retained during the restoration process.

## Lessons Learned
- Users should be clearly informed about the implications of service expiration notifications.
- Users should be proactive in renewing services, especially if they are not automatically extended.
- HPC Admins should provide clear guidelines on account restoration and data retention policies.

## Next Steps
- HPC Admins to review the user's application and determine if data can be retained.
- Provide clear communication to the user regarding the status of their account and data.

## References
- [RRZE Service Counters](https://www.rrze.fau.de/infocenter/kontakt-hilfe/service-theken/)
- [IdM Portal](www.idm.fau.de)
---

### 2023090142000379_Umstellung%20Tier3-HPC-Versorgung%20der%20Uni-Bayreuth%20am%20RRZE%20auf%20elektronisches%20HPC-Port.md
# Ticket 2023090142000379

 # HPC Support Ticket: Transition to Electronic HPC Portal for Uni-Bayreuth

## Keywords
- Tier3-HPC-Ressourcen
- HPC-Portal
- SSO via DFN/AAI
- GPGPU-Ressourcen
- NVlink
- Manager-Rechte
- SSH-Key
- NHR-Versorgung

## Summary
The HPC Admin proposed a transition from paper-based account creation to an electronic HPC portal for Uni-Bayreuth users. The transition aims to streamline the process and reduce administrative overhead.

## Problem
- Current system uses paper forms and an IdM-System account for Tier3-HPC-Accounts.
- Inefficient and cumbersome process for a small number of users.

## Solution
- Transition to a modern, electronic HPC portal.
- Single Sign-On (SSO) via DFN/AAI for account creation.
- Delegate administration to a local manager from the Uni-Bayreuth Rechenzentrum or HPC group.

## Steps Taken
1. **HPC Admin**: Proposed the transition and outlined the new process.
2. **User**: Agreed to take on the role of local manager and registered on the HPC portal.

## New Process
- **Account Creation**:
  - Users register via SSO.
  - Local manager invites users and manages account expiration dates.
- **Project Structure**:
  - xxxx100: Grundversorgung (employees, doctoral students, scholarship holders, student assistants, etc.)
  - xxxx101: Studentische Abschlußarbeiten (Bachelor/Master students, not PhD students)
  - xxxx102: Projektpartner (external users not employed by the institution)
- **Access**:
  - SSH access via SSH-Key uploaded through the HPC portal.
  - No separate passwords for portal HPC accounts.

## Documentation
- General documentation for NHR projects applies to Tier3, with the local manager replacing the PI.
- [NHR@FAU HPC Portal Usage](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/documentation-instructions/getting-started/nhrfau-hpc-portal-usage/)

## Conclusion
The transition to the electronic HPC portal aims to simplify account management and improve user experience. The local manager at Uni-Bayreuth will handle invitations, account expiration, and serve as the primary contact for HPC support.
---

### 2020060542002175_Antrag%20HPC.md
# Ticket 2020060542002175

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC access request
- Master student
- Form signature
- HPC account setup
- Password setup
- HPC-Café
- MPI-parallel code
- Checkpoint-restart
- LRZ-Linuxcluster

## Summary
- **User Request:** Access to HPC for a Master student in Prof. Sasaki's group.
- **Issue:** Difficulty in obtaining a physical signature due to current circumstances.
- **Action Taken:** HPC Admins created an HPC account (`mpai001h`) and provided instructions for password setup.
- **Additional Information:** User was invited to the HPC-Café for further support.
- **Root Cause:** Lack of awareness about HPC resources in Erlangen, leading to an initial plan to use the LRZ-Linuxcluster.
- **Solution:** Provided access to HPC resources and guidance on setting up the account.

## Lessons Learned
- **Signature Issues:** In cases where physical signatures are difficult to obtain, proxy signatures can be accepted with proper authorization.
- **Account Setup:** New users need to set up their passwords through the IDM portal and wait for the services to be initialized on HPC servers.
- **User Support:** Regular events like the HPC-Café can provide valuable support for new users.
- **Resource Awareness:** Ensure users are aware of available HPC resources to avoid unnecessary delays or misuse of other clusters.

## Recommendations
- **Documentation:** Update documentation to include steps for proxy signatures and account setup.
- **Communication:** Regularly communicate available resources and support events to users.
- **Training:** Encourage participation in training sessions and support events to enhance user knowledge and efficiency.

---

This report provides a concise overview of the support ticket conversation, highlighting key actions, issues, and solutions. It serves as a reference for future support cases involving similar issues.
---

### 2024101742003181_Testprojekt%20e104ef%20_%20JA-23543%20DCBT%20-%20Droplet%20Coalescence%20and%20Breakup%20in%20Turbul.md
# Ticket 2024101742003181

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- JARDS
- UPLOAD SIGNED FORM
- SSO (DFN-AAI/eduGAIN)
- HPC portal
- SSH keys
- Home directory
- Technical review
- Compute time application
- NHR@FAU

## General Learnings
- **JARDS Usability**: JARDS can be non-intuitive, and users may struggle to find certain functionalities like the "UPLOAD SIGNED FORM" button.
- **SSO Login**: Users need to log in via SSO to the HPC portal to be connected to the project.
- **SSH Keys**: Access to HPC systems is exclusively via SSH keys, which must be uploaded by each user in the HPC portal.
- **Home Directory**: Home directories are created automatically but may take some time to be available.
- **Technical Review**: The technical review process involves multiple steps and may require input from different team members.

## Root Cause of Problems
- **Missing UPLOAD SIGNED FORM Button**: The user could not find the "UPLOAD SIGNED FORM" button in JARDS.
- **Home Directory Error**: The user encountered an error due to a missing home directory, which was expected to be created automatically.

## Solutions
- **Alternative Submission**: If the "UPLOAD SIGNED FORM" button is not found, users can send the signed form via email.
- **Home Directory Creation**: Home directories are created automatically, but it may take up to a day for them to be available.

## Documentation for Support Employees
### JARDS Usability
- **Issue**: Users may struggle to find the "UPLOAD SIGNED FORM" button in JARDS.
- **Solution**: Advise users to send the signed form via email if they cannot find the button.

### SSO Login
- **Issue**: Users need to log in via SSO to the HPC portal.
- **Solution**: Ensure users are aware of the SSO login requirement and provide instructions if needed.

### SSH Keys
- **Issue**: Access to HPC systems requires SSH keys.
- **Solution**: Instruct users to upload their SSH keys in the HPC portal.

### Home Directory
- **Issue**: Users may encounter errors due to missing home directories.
- **Solution**: Inform users that home directories are created automatically and may take up to a day to be available.

### Technical Review
- **Issue**: The technical review process involves multiple steps.
- **Solution**: Ensure all necessary steps are completed and communicate the progress to the user.
```
---

### 2017050542000952_HPC-Accounts%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20%C3%83%C2%9Cbungsbetrieb.md

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket: HPC-Accounts für Übungsbetrieb

- Course Support

## Root Cause
- Expired certificate mentioned in the initial message.
- Need for HPC accounts for students for the duration of the course.

2. **Account Creation:** HPC Admins created HPC accounts for the students with the identifiers `pacl000h` to `pacl022h`.
3. **Notification:** Students were informed that they could find their HPC identifiers in the IDM-Portal and reset their passwords if needed.

## Solution
- HPC Admins created the necessary HPC accounts for the students.
- Students were directed to the IDM-Portal to access their accounts and reset passwords if required.

## General Learnings
- Professors can request HPC accounts for their students by providing a list and duration.
- HPC Admins handle the creation of these accounts and notify the students.
- Students can manage their HPC accounts through the IDM-Portal, including password resets.

## Follow-Up
- Ensure that the students are aware of the account creation and how to access the IDM-Portal.
- Monitor the accounts to ensure they are active for the specified duration.
```

### 2024111542001149_Fragen%20zu%20Zugang%20und%20%22free%20basic%20usage%22.md
# Ticket 2024111542001149

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation: Access and "Free Basic Usage"

## Keywords
- HPC Access
- Tier3 Grundversorgung
- Exportkontrollbestimmungen
- HPC-Portal
- SSO Login
- Limits
- Lizenzpflichtige Software
- HPC-Cafe
- Manager-Rechte

## Problem
- A student at the Chair of Construction Technology may need HPC access.
- The user is unsure about the steps required to enable HPC usage.
- The user wants to know if the RRZE contact person can apply for HPC access.
- The user inquires about the current limits for "Tier3 Grundversorgung" or "free basic usage."

## Solution
- The chair holder or PI must log in to the HPC-Portal via SSO and confirm the export control regulations. This step cannot be delegated.
- If the RRZE contact person is to handle HPC-related tasks, the PI must confirm this via email.
- Limits for "Tier3 Grundversorgung" are not fixed and vary based on overall FAU usage. Exceeding limits results in longer wait times in the Slurm queue.
- For licensed software, users must provide the necessary network licenses.
- Monthly introductory sessions for new HPC users are available.

## General Information
- The HPC-Portal is used for managing HPC accounts and projects.
- Different project categories are available for various user groups (e.g., employees, students, external partners).
- The HPC-Portal offers advantages such as electronic invitations, self-management of account lifetimes, and usage statistics.
- Additional resources and FAQs are available on the HPC documentation website.

## Root Cause
- The user was unaware of the specific steps required to enable HPC access and the role of the PI in confirming export control regulations.
- The user needed clarification on the limits and usage of licensed software within the "Tier3 Grundversorgung."

## Resolution
- The HPC Admin provided detailed instructions on the necessary steps for enabling HPC access, including the requirement for the PI to log in and confirm export control regulations.
- The HPC Admin explained the flexible nature of limits within the "Tier3 Grundversorgung" and the potential for longer wait times if limits are exceeded.
- The HPC Admin highlighted the need for users to provide network licenses for licensed software and offered resources for further information and support.

This documentation can be used to assist other users with similar inquiries about HPC access and usage limits.
---

### 2021042042003085_HPC%20Antr%C3%83%C2%A4ge.md
# Ticket 2021042042003085

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC Anträge (HPC Applications)
- Certificate expiration
- HPC-Kennung (HPC Identification)
- IdM-Kennung (Identity Management Identification)
- HPC-Café
- Password setup
- Service initialization
- GPU hours
- Storage allocation

## General Learnings
- **User Request**: Submission of HPC applications for processing.
- **Issue**: Certificate expiration mentioned in the initial request.
- **HPC Admin Actions**:
  - Created HPC identifications for users.
  - Provided instructions for setting up passwords and initializing services.
  - Recommended participation in the HPC-Café for new users.
  - Noted that service initialization can take up to 2 days.
  - Closed the ticket after processing the applications.
- **Solution**:
  - Users were instructed to log in to the IdM portal to set up their passwords.
  - Users were informed about the delay in service initialization.
  - Users were encouraged to participate in the HPC-Café for additional support.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- Certificate expiration was mentioned but not addressed in the conversation.

## Solution
- No specific solution provided for the certificate expiration issue.
- Users were guided through the process of setting up their HPC accounts and services.

## Documentation for Support Employees
When handling HPC application requests:
1. **Create HPC Identifications**: Assign HPC-Kennung to users.
2. **Password Setup**: Instruct users to set up their passwords via the IdM portal.
3. **Service Initialization**: Inform users about the delay in service initialization.
4. **Recommend HPC-Café**: Encourage new users to participate in the HPC-Café for additional support.
5. **Address Certificate Issues**: If certificate expiration is mentioned, provide guidance on renewing or updating certificates.

This documentation can be used to handle similar requests and ensure users are properly onboarded to the HPC system.
---

 # HPC Support Ticket Analysis

- Woody cluster

## Summary
A user from the Biocomputing AG at the chair of Biochemistry requests access to the HPC clusters (Meggie, Woody) but lacks a technical contact with credentials to create accounts. The HPC Admin identifies that the project is already set up but requires the professor's login to be fully functional.

## Root Cause
- Lack of a technical contact with credentials to create accounts.
- Project setup is incomplete due to the professor not logging into the HPC portal.

## Solution
- The user is advised to ask the professor to follow the steps outlined in the provided link to complete the project setup.

## General Learnings
- Ensure that the designated technical contact has the necessary credentials to create accounts.
- Projects in the HPC system require the professor's login to be fully functional.
- Users should follow the official application process for creating new projects on the HPC portal.

## Next Steps for Support
- Verify if the professor has completed the login process.
- Assist the user in becoming the technical contact if the professor's login is confirmed.
- Provide further guidance on account creation and cluster usage if needed.
---

### 2022111542002473_Re%3A%20Manager%20acces%20granted%20for%22FLINSENOI%20-%20Flow%20induce%20self-noise%22%20at%20porta.md
# Ticket 2022111542002473

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Summary

## Keywords
- Large-scale project proposal
- Manager access
- HPC portal
- SSH keys
- OpenFOAM module
- NHR accounts
- Monitoring portal
- $WORK variable

## General Learnings
- Large-scale projects require approval from the central usage committee of the NHR association.
- Manager access to projects is granted via the HPC portal.
- New accounts for large-scale projects are created through the portal and require SSH keys for access.
- OpenFOAM module issues can arise due to permission problems with the $WORK variable.
- The monitoring portal can be accessed through a link in the HPC portal.

## Root Causes and Solutions

### Issue: Manager Access and Portal Usage
- **Root Cause**: User uncertainty about manager access and portal usage.
- **Solution**: HPC Admins granted manager access and provided instructions for inviting users and reviewing accounts through the portal.

### Issue: SSH Key and Account Creation
- **Root Cause**: User needed to generate SSH keys and upload them to the portal.
- **Solution**: User generated and uploaded SSH keys, and accounts were created the next day.

### Issue: OpenFOAM Module Permission Error
- **Root Cause**: Permission denied error when creating a directory in `/home/woody`.
- **Solution**: HPC Admins changed the module to use `/home/$env(WORK)` instead of `/home/woody`, resolving the permission issue.

### Issue: Monitoring Portal Access
- **Root Cause**: Direct login to the monitoring portal not possible.
- **Solution**: Access the monitoring portal through a link in the HPC portal that transmits login credentials.

## Documentation Links
- [HPC Portal Usage](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/documentation-instructions/getting-started/nhrfau-hpc-portal-usage/)
- [Monitoring Portal](https://monitoring.nhr.fau.de/)

## Notes
- Accounts are available on the systems the morning after accepting the invitation.
- SSH keys take 2 hours to be recognized on all systems after upload.
- Additional software (Ansys, STAR-CCM+) was enabled for the project as needed.
---

### 2024021642002565_Vorbereitungen%20zur%20Umstellung%20der%20iwih-HPC-Accounts%20von%20Papier-HPC-Antr%C3%83%C2%A4gen%2.md
# Ticket 2024021642002565

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Summary

## Subject
Vorbereitungen zur Umstellung der iwih-HPC-Accounts von Papier-HPC-Anträgen auf das online HPC-Portal

## Keywords
- HPC-Portal
- SSO-Login
- Export-Kontrollbestimmungen
- Manager-Rechte
- HPC-Kennungen
- Projektkategorien
- SSH-Keys
- ClusterCockpit
- Jupyterhub

## General Learnings
- The HPC-Portal is being introduced to replace paper-based HPC applications.
- Principal Investigators (PIs) need to log in to the HPC-Portal and confirm export control regulations.
- Managers can be assigned to handle day-to-day operations.
- HPC accounts will be categorized into different project categories.
- SSH-Keys will be used for secure access, replacing passwords.
- The HPC-Portal will provide various advantages, including easier account management and enhanced security.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- The PI has not logged in to the HPC-Portal to confirm the export control regulations.

## Solution
- The HPC Admin has extended the HPC accounts until October and will proceed with the import into the HPC-Portal.
- The accounts will be imported without a manager, which will be addressed during any necessary extensions.

## Actions Taken
- HPC Admin informed the PI about the transition to the HPC-Portal.
- HPC Admin extended the HPC accounts until October.
- HPC Admin will import the accounts into the HPC-Portal and set the IdM expiration to October 15.
- HPC Admin will send a user email to inform about the changes.

## Next Steps
- The PI needs to log in to the HPC-Portal and confirm the export control regulations.
- Managers need to be assigned for day-to-day operations.
- The HPC accounts will be imported into the HPC-Portal, and users will be informed separately.

## Additional Information
- The HPC-Portal has been in use for NHR projects for almost two years and has proven to be robust and functional.
- The use of SSH-Keys enhances security by requiring both a passphrase and the key itself.
- ClusterCockpit and Jupyterhub will be accessible via Single Sign-on through the HPC-Portal.

## References
- [HPC-Portal Usage](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/documentation-instructions/getting-started/nhrfau-hpc-portal-usage/)
- [SSH Secure Shell Access](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/documentation-instructions/ssh-secure-shell-access-to-hpc-systems/)
- [FAQs on SSH](https://hpc.fau.de/faqs/#ID-230)

## Support Team
- **HPC Admins**: Thomas Zeiser, Michael Meier, Anna Kahler, Katrin Nusser, Johannes Veh
- **2nd Level Support Team**: Lacey, Dane (fo36fizy), Kuckuk, Sebastian (sisekuck), Lange, Florian (ow86apyf), Ernst, Dominik (te42kyfo), Mayr, Martin
- **Head of the Datacenter**: Gerhard Wellein
- **Training and Support Group Leader**: Georg Hager
- **NHR Rechenzeit Support and Applications for Grants**: Harald Lanig
- **Software and Tools Developer**: Jan Eitzinger, Gruber
---

### 42226030_Anmeldung%20auf%20woody.md
# Ticket 42226030

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Anmeldung auf woody

## Keywords
- Password change
- SSH login
- IdM-Portal
- woody cluster
- Password synchronization delay

## Problem
- User changed password on IdM-Portal but could not log in via SSH to the woody cluster.

## Root Cause
- Delay in synchronization of password changes from the IdM-Portal to the HPC clusters.

## Solution
- Wait for the synchronization process to complete, which can take up to a day.
- If the issue persists, try changing the password again.

## General Learnings
- Password changes made on the IdM-Portal may take some time to propagate to the HPC clusters.
- If a password change does not reflect on the cluster, retrying the password change may resolve the issue.
- The old password hash can be used as a reference to confirm if the change has been processed.
```
---

### 42329324_MIC%20cards%20on%20emmy.md
# Ticket 42329324

 # HPC Support Ticket: MIC Cards Access Issue on Emmy

## Keywords
- MIC cards
- Account expiration
- User database synchronization
- Reboot
- Emmy cluster

## Problem Description
- **User Issue:** Unable to access MIC cards on Emmy cluster.
- **Error Message:** "Your account has expired; please contact your system administrator. Connection closed by 10.28.8.172"
- **Root Cause:** Configuration problem with MICs not picking up the new user database, leading to unrecognized new or recently prolonged accounts.

## Solution
- **Action Taken:** HPC Admins decided to reboot all MICs to synchronize the user database.
- **Resolution:** After the reboot, the user was able to access the MICs again.

## General Learnings
- MIC cards may not synchronize with the user database automatically, requiring manual intervention such as a reboot.
- New or recently prolonged accounts may face access issues due to outdated user databases on MICs.
- Rebooting MICs can resolve synchronization issues but may take some time to complete.

## Follow-up
- Notify the user once the MICs are usable again after the reboot.
- Ensure that the user database synchronization process is monitored to prevent similar issues in the future.
---

### 2015112442002102_Re%3A%20HPC-Account%20mptf07%20am_at%20RRZE.md
# Ticket 2015112442002102

 # HPC-Support Ticket: Password Hash Update

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Password Hash
- SSHA512
- IDM Portal
- Migration

## Summary
- **Root Cause**: User's password hash was outdated and needed to be updated to SSHA512 to prevent login issues after the upcoming system migration.
- **Solution**: User was instructed to change their password through the IDM portal. The status "migrating" was clarified, and the hash update was planned for the first working week of January.

## Detailed Steps
1. **User Issue**: The user had not changed their HPC account password for several years, leading to an outdated password hash.
2. **Admin Instruction**: The user was asked to log into the IDM portal and change their password to update the hash to SSHA512.
3. **User Action**: The user changed their password but encountered a "migrating" status.
4. **Admin Clarification**: The "migrating" status was explained to be related to the database account, not the HPC account. The hash update was scheduled for the first working week of January.
5. **Resolution**: The user successfully updated their password, resolving the issue.

## General Learnings
- Regular password updates are crucial to maintain secure and compatible password hashes.
- The IDM portal allows users to change their passwords without enforcing a new one.
- The "migrating" status in the IDM portal can be resolved by resetting the password for the relevant account.

## Future Reference
- Instruct users to update their passwords regularly to avoid hash-related login issues.
- Clarify the significance of the "migrating" status and its resolution through password reset.
- Plan and communicate system updates, such as hash algorithm changes, well in advance to ensure user preparedness.
---

### 2024111742000361_create%20account%20without%20SSO-sign%20in.md
# Ticket 2024111742000361

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Create Account Without SSO-Sign In

## Summary
A user attempted to set up an account for a researcher from KAUST but encountered an error. The ticket highlights issues related to export control regulations and technical problems.

## Keywords
- Account creation
- SSO-sign in
- Export control
- Technical issue
- KAUST
- Formal review

## Problem
- User encountered an error while setting up an account for a researcher from KAUST.
- The error was documented with screenshots ([73468719-4f48-41d0-8d29-4ec64a379299.jpeg], [ad5b7a95-5b74-415e-9981-4da34a23ccba.jpeg]).

## Root Cause
- Technical issue with account creation.
- Potential export control regulations affecting account creation for researchers from certain institutions or nationalities.

## Solution
- The technical issue needs to be addressed by KAUST.
- Account creation for researchers from KAUST requires formal review due to export control regulations.
- The review process may involve the BaFa (Federal Office of Economics and Export Control).

## Additional Information
- An informational event on export control and HPC was held (Monthly HPC Café: Exportkontrolle & HPC, November 12, hybrid event, German).
- The user was advised to attend such events for better understanding of the regulations.

## Conclusion
- Technical issues with account creation should be resolved by the user's institution.
- Compliance with export control regulations is mandatory and requires formal review before account creation.
```
---

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Zugang zu Cluster nicht möglich

## Keywords
- HPC-Zugang
- IdM-Kennung
- HPC-Kennung
- Woody-Cluster
- Passwort
- IDM
- Frontends

## Problem
- User unable to log in to the Woody-Cluster.
- User's HPC access appears to be deactivated.
- User attempted to log in with an incorrect password and then changed the password in IDM.

## Root Cause
- The new password set by the user was not distributed to the frontends.
- The user attempted to log in before the new password was synchronized.

## Solution
- The user should attempt to log in again using the password set at 13:34 the previous day.

## General Learning
- Ensure that password changes in IDM are properly synchronized with the frontends before attempting to log in.
- Verify that the correct password is being used for login attempts.
```
---

### 2016032142002381_Woody%20access%20denied.md
# Ticket 2016032142002381

 # HPC Support Ticket: Woody Access Denied

## Keywords
- Access Denied
- Woody
- WinSCP
- Keyboard-interactive authentication
- Password propagation
- IDM account
- HPC account

## Problem Description
- User encountered access issues with Woody (woodycap1 from ECAP) after reinstalling WinSCP.
- Attempted login with different hostnames (woody.rrze.uni-erlangen.de, woodycap1.rrze.uni-erlangen.de, woody3.rrze.uni-erlangen.de) and usernames (in57arek, si0301).
- Received "Using keyboard-interactive authentication" message and "access denied" errors.

## Root Cause
- Incorrect password usage: User might be using the IDM account password instead of the HPC account password.
- Password propagation delay: Recent password changes might not have been propagated to the HPC systems yet.

## Solution
- Ensure the user is using the correct password for their HPC account, not the IDM account.
- If the password was recently changed, advise the user to wait several hours for the changes to propagate to the HPC systems.

## Troubleshooting Steps
- Check the user's account validity and password hash.
- Verify login attempts and failure messages on relevant servers (e.g., woody3, woodycap3).
- Confirm the user is aware of the password propagation delay after changing the HPC password.

## Follow-up
- If the issue persists, escalate to HPC Admins for further investigation.

## Related Parties
- HPC Admins
- 2nd Level Support Team
- Head of the Datacenter
- Training and Support Group Leader
- NHR Rechenzeit Support
- Software and Tools Developer
---

### 2021051842000411_Ablaufendes%20Konto.md
# Ticket 2021051842000411

 # HPC Support Ticket: Expiring Account

## Keywords
- Account expiration
- Account renewal
- HPC application form
- IDM system
- Zugehörigkeit (affiliation)

## Problem
- User received a notification from the IDM system that their HPC account is expiring.
- User is transitioning from a Master's student to a PhD student at the same institution.

## Root Cause
- The user's account was set to expire based on their current status as a Master's student.

## Solution
1. **Account Renewal Application**: The user was instructed to fill out a new application form for account renewal.
   - **Form Link**: [HPC Application Form](https://www.rrze.fau.de/files/2017/06/HPC-Antrag.pdf)
   - **Instructions**:
     - Select "Verlängerung" (renewal) at the top.
     - Set the HPC expiration date to be linked to the current affiliation as an employee/PhD student.
2. **Submission**: The user submitted the renewal application via email.
3. **Admin Action**: The HPC admin updated the account's permissions to be linked to the user's new affiliation as an employee/PhD student.

## General Learning
- Users transitioning to a new academic status should renew their HPC accounts by submitting a new application form.
- The account's expiration date should be linked to the user's current affiliation to avoid manual renewals.
- The IDM system tracks user affiliations and account statuses, triggering notifications for expiring accounts.
---

### 2023062942001714_recover%20master%20student%27s%20data.md
# Ticket 2023062942001714

 # HPC Support Ticket: Recover Master Student's Data

## Keywords
- Data recovery
- Account permissions
- Directory access
- Expired account

## Problem
- User needs access to data produced by a former master student.
- The former student's account is no longer active.
- User does not have permissions to access the student's directories.

## Root Cause
- The former student's account has expired, preventing access to their data.
- User lacks necessary permissions to access the required directories.

## Solution
1. **Permission Request**: The user must obtain permission from the former student to access their data.
2. **Email Confirmation**: The former student sends an email to HPC support granting access.
3. **HPC Admin Action**: Upon receiving the email, HPC admins change the directory permissions to allow the user access.

## Outcome
- The user was granted access to the directory `/home/saturn/capn/mppi107h`.
- Data in `/home/wecapstor3` and `/home/woody` were already deleted and could not be recovered.

## General Learnings
- Always ensure data is backed up or transferred to a shared directory before a user's account expires.
- Permissions for accessing data must be explicitly granted by the data owner.
- HPC admins require formal confirmation before changing directory permissions.
---

### 2018040442000883_Changing%20the%20password%20in%20HPC.md
# Ticket 2018040442000883

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket Conversation: Changing the Password in HPC

## Keywords
- Password change
- HPC cluster access
- IDM portal
- SSH login
- Forgotten password

## Summary
A user requested information on changing their password for accessing the HPC clusters Emmy and Lima, as they had forgotten their previous password and were unable to log in.

## Root Cause
- User forgot their HPC password.
- User attempted to change the password but encountered issues logging in via SSH.

## Solution
- **Password Change Instructions**: The user was instructed to change their password through the IDM portal (https://idm.fau.de). They needed to log in with their main IDM account, not their HPC account. The HPC account would be listed under Profile -> Data overview (scroll down to "Other Services").
- **IDM Password Recovery**: If the user had also forgotten their IDM password, they were advised to use the recovery mechanisms available on the IDM portal.

## Additional Notes
- The user attempted to change their password but still faced login issues.
- The HPC admin provided detailed instructions on how to change the password via the IDM portal.

## General Learning
- Users should be aware of the IDM portal for managing their HPC account passwords.
- If users forget their IDM password, they should use the recovery mechanisms provided on the IDM portal.
- HPC admins should guide users through the password change process and ensure they understand the steps involved.
```
---

### 2024110442002865_Frage%20zu%20Daten%20auf%20ehemaligem%20Account%20tumu001h.md
# Ticket 2024110442002865

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Subject
Frage zu Daten auf ehemaligem Account tumu001h

## Keywords
- Data recovery
- Account closure
- Backup policy
- HPC support
- Simulation data
- Alex-Cluster
- NHR@FAU

## What Can Be Learned

### User Issue
- User generated simulation data in June 2022 on the Alex-Cluster (account tumu001h).
- Account was closed in 2022.
- User did not download all data to their local machine.
- User inquires about the possibility of recovering the data from a backup.

### HPC Admin Response
- Accounts are deleted 3 months after expiration.
- Data recovery from > 2-year-old accounts is challenging.
- No backup exists for the work directory (atuin) and other $WORK servers.

### Solution
- Data recovery from the specified filesystem is not possible as the directory has been deleted and no backup exists.

### General Learnings
- **Account Closure Policy**: Accounts are deleted 3 months after expiration.
- **Backup Policy**: No backup exists for the work directory and other $WORK servers.
- **Data Management**: Users should ensure they download important data before account closure.

## Conclusion
Users should be aware of the account closure and backup policies to avoid data loss. If data recovery is needed, it is essential to act promptly and provide specific details about the filesystem used.

---

This analysis provides insights into the data recovery process and the importance of understanding HPC policies regarding account closure and backups.
---

### 2017092142001879_Antrag%20auf%20HPC-Berechtigung.md

- Expired certificate

4. **Service Setup**: The user was informed that it may take up to 2 days for the services on the HPC servers to be fully set up.

## Notes
- The application was initially delayed due to an expired certificate.
- The HPC Admin team provided detailed instructions for setting up the new HPC-Kennung.
- The user was advised to wait for up to 2 days for the services to be fully operational.

## Follow-Up
- Ensure the user follows the instructions for setting up the password.
- Verify that the services are operational within the specified timeframe.
- Update the user on the status of the HPC authorization if any delays occur.

### 2020101542002666_Cannot%20log%20in%20to%20woody.md
# Ticket 2020101542002666

 # HPC Support Ticket: Cannot Log In to Woody

## Keywords
- HPC cluster access
- Password reset
- IDM portal
- Password propagation delay

## Problem Description
- User unable to log in to the HPC cluster "woody" despite having the correct credentials.
- Password change via IDM portal did not resolve the issue immediately.

## Root Cause
- Password propagation delay across systems.

## Solution
- Wait for the new password to propagate through all systems, which may take a few hours up to one day.
- If the issue persists after the expected propagation time, contact HPC support again.

## General Learnings
- Changing the password via the IDM portal is the correct method for HPC clusters.
- Password changes may not be immediate and can take up to one day to propagate.
- Users should be informed about the potential delay in password propagation to avoid confusion and repeated attempts.

## Next Steps for Support
- Inform users about the expected delay in password propagation when they change their passwords.
- Monitor the system for any unusual delays in password propagation and address them promptly.
---

### 2023061242000809_HPC%20Zugang%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20Kurs.md
# Ticket 2023061242000809

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Summary

## Subject: HPC Zugang für Kurs

### Keywords:
- Temporary HPC access
- Student accounts
- Module installation
- Woody-ng
- IDM Portal
- Unix-Gruppe "rzku"

### General Learnings:
- Process for requesting temporary HPC access for students.
- Handling module installation requests.
- Communication between users and HPC admins.

### Root Cause of the Problem:
- User requested temporary HPC access for students for a course.
- User noticed that the "parallel" module was not available on Woody-ng.

### Solution:
- HPC admins generated temporary accounts with a specified expiration date.
- HPC admins installed the "parallel" module as an OS-Paket on Woody-ng.

### Detailed Steps:
1. **User Request:**
   - User requested temporary HPC access for students for the course "Scripting for Remote Sensing".
   - Provided IDM Kennungen for the students.

2. **HPC Admin Response:**
   - HPC admins generated temporary accounts (gwgi029h-gwgi047h) with a validity until the end of September.
   - Accounts were added to the Unix-Gruppe "rzku" for educational purposes.

3. **User Follow-up:**
   - User noticed that the "parallel" module was not available on Woody-ng.
   - Requested the installation of the module.

4. **HPC Admin Follow-up:**
   - HPC admins installed the "parallel" module as an OS-Paket on Woody-ng.

### Notes:
- The bulk function in the IDM Portal generated different account prefixes than in previous years.
- Communication was maintained through email, with users and admins providing updates and confirmations.

### Conclusion:
- The process for handling temporary HPC access requests and module installations was successfully completed.
- Users and HPC admins collaborated to ensure all necessary resources were available for the course.
---

### 2024042342002431_Question%20concerning%20HPC%20Account%20-%20Basic%20TIER3%20Project%20-%20PI%20role%20-%20F.%20Graw%.md
# Ticket 2024042342002431

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Subject
Question concerning HPC Account - Basic TIER3 Project - PI role

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Tier3 Project
- PI Role
- HPC Portal
- RRZE KuNu-Präfix
- Manager Rights
- Organizational Unit
- HPC Clusters
- File Systems
- Data Transfer
- SSH
- Introduction for New Users
- HPC-Portal
- Single Sign-on (SSO)
- Export-Kontrollbestimmungen
- Projektpartner
- SSH-Keys
- ClusterCockpit
- Jupyterhub

## Summary
A professor from the Department of Medicine at FAU requested information on initiating a Tier3 project and inquired about the PI role. The HPC Admin provided detailed instructions on the process, including the creation of projects, manager rights, and additional resources.

## Root Cause of the Problem
The user needed guidance on initiating a Tier3 project and understanding the PI role within the HPC system.

## Solution
The HPC Admin provided comprehensive information on the steps required to initiate the project, including:
- Confirming export control regulations via the HPC portal.
- Assigning a "RRZE KuNu-Präfix" to the professorship.
- Creating three standard projects for the organizational unit.
- Granting manager rights to the PI.
- Providing links to resources for HPC clusters, file systems, data transfer, SSH, and introductory sessions.

## General Learnings
- The HPC portal is used for managing HPC accounts and projects.
- Projects are created per organizational unit, with individual accounts within each project.
- Manager rights are assigned to the PI, who can delegate tasks to other team members.
- Regular introductory sessions are offered for new users.
- The HPC portal provides various features such as account management, statistics, and access to additional services like ClusterCockpit and Jupyterhub.
- SSH-Keys are recommended for enhanced security.

## Additional Resources
- [HPC Clusters](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/clusters/)
- [File Systems](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/data/filesystems/)
- [Data Transfer](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/data/copying/)
- [SSH Command Line](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/access/ssh-command-line/)
- [HPC Portal](https://portal.hpc.fau.de)
- [Introduction for New Users](https://hpc.fau.de/teaching/hpc-cafe/)
- [ClusterCockpit](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/job-monitoring-with-clustercockpit/)
```
---

### 2024101742001754_Re%3A%20Your%20account%20%22mpm317%22%20for%20project%20%22Tier3%20Grundversorgung%20LS%20Angewandte.md
# Ticket 2024101742001754

 # HPC Support Ticket: Account Expiration and Extension Process

## Keywords
- Account expiration
- Account extension
- Export control regulations
- HPC portal
- Project manager
- PI (Principal Investigator)

## Summary
A user's HPC account was about to expire, and they inquired about the extension process. The extension required the project's PI to log into the HPC portal and accept export control regulations.

## Root Cause
- The PI had not logged into the HPC portal to accept the export control regulations, which is a mandatory step for account extension.

## Steps Taken
1. **Notification**: The user was notified about the account expiration via email.
2. **Inquiry**: The user asked about the extension process.
3. **Response**: HPC Admins informed the user that the PI needs to log into the HPC portal and accept the export control regulations.
4. **Follow-up**: The user confirmed that the export control regulations were accepted.
5. **Resolution**: HPC Admins granted the PI manager rights to extend account durations and manage new invitations.

## Solution
- The PI must log into the HPC portal and accept the export control regulations.
- Once accepted, the PI can manage account extensions and new invitations.

## General Learnings
- Account extensions require the PI to accept export control regulations.
- Manager rights can be granted to the PI for handling account durations and invitations.
- Clear communication and follow-up are essential for resolving account-related issues.

## References
- [HPC Portal Documentation](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/)
- HPC Support Email: support-hpc@fau.de

## Roles Involved
- **HPC Admins**: Provided guidance and granted manager rights.
- **User**: Inquired about the extension process and followed the instructions.
- **PI**: Accepted the export control regulations and managed account extensions.

## Status
- Resolved: The PI accepted the regulations, and manager rights were granted for account management.
---

### 2025012042003592_Frage%20zu%20HPC%20Nutzung%20-%20Institut%20ELSYS_TH-N%C3%83%C2%BCrnberg%20%3D%3E%20BayernKI.md
# Ticket 2025012042003592

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC Nutzung
- TH Nürnberg
- Basic Supply Project
- Rechenleistung
- Forschungsprojekt
- Account Management
- Exportkontrollbestimmungen
- BayernKI
- RRZE Service Area

## Summary
A research assistant from the ELSYS Institute at TH Nürnberg inquired about using HPC resources from FAU. The conversation revolved around the process of setting up an HPC project for the institute.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- The user was unsure about the procedure for accessing HPC resources as a member of the RRZE Service Area.
- There was confusion about whether a basic supply project exists for TH Nürnberg or if a formal application for research project resources is required.

## Solution
- HPC Admins clarified that while there are existing projects for TH Nürnberg, it would be simpler to create a dedicated project for ELSYS.
- A professor from ELSYS needed to contact HPC Admins to initiate the project setup.
- The project was to be managed through BayernKI for billing purposes, with no disadvantages for the user.
- The professor was required to register at the HPC portal and accept export control regulations.

## Steps Taken
1. **Initial Inquiry**: The user asked about the process for accessing HPC resources.
2. **Clarification by HPC Admins**: HPC Admins explained the need for a dedicated project for ELSYS and the requirement for a professor to initiate the process.
3. **Form Submission**: The professor registered and submitted the necessary form.
4. **Project Setup**: HPC Admins created the project and linked the professor as the Principal Investigator (PI).

## General Learnings
- Members of the RRZE Service Area can access HPC resources by setting up a dedicated project.
- The process involves a professor initiating the project and accepting export control regulations.
- Projects may be managed through BayernKI for billing purposes without any disadvantages to the user.

## Conclusion
The ticket was resolved by setting up a dedicated project for ELSYS, with the professor as the PI. The process involved registration and acceptance of export control regulations.
```
---

### 2022052042001403_Retrieving%20data%20for%20old%20HPC%20account.md
# Ticket 2022052042001403

 # Retrieving Data for Expired HPC Account

## Keywords
- Expired HPC account
- Data retrieval
- Account reactivation
- Retention period
- Backup

## Problem
- User's HPC account expired, and they forgot to backup some files.
- User needs to retrieve data from the expired account.

## Root Cause
- User did not backup data before account expiration.

## Solution
- HPC Admin reactivated the account for 1 week to allow the user to login and backup data.
- User was instructed to also remove data they no longer need.

## General Learnings
- There is a 3-month retention period for expired accounts.
- HPC Admin can temporarily reactivate expired accounts for data retrieval.
- Always backup important data before account expiration.
- Remove unnecessary data to free up storage space.
---

### 2025031442001333_Fritz%20Access%20for%20MA%20Student.md
# Ticket 2025031442001333

 # HPC-Support Ticket: Fritz Access for MA Student

## Keywords
- Fritz access
- MA student
- Account expiration
- ihpc account
- Project invitation

## Problem
- A new MA student requires Fritz access for at least one year.
- The student's current account (`muco133v`) is set to expire in April.

## Root Cause
- The student is using a temporary account that will expire soon.

## Solution
- MA students should use an ihpc account.
- The supervisor (2nd Level Support team member) should request GHa or JE to send an invitation for the project.
- Once the account is created (within 24 hours after the invitation is accepted), HPC Admins can grant access to Fritz.

## General Learnings
- MA students should be provided with ihpc accounts for long-term access.
- Project invitations are necessary for account creation.
- Account creation takes approximately 24 hours after invitation acceptance.
- HPC Admins handle granting access to specific resources like Fritz.

## Follow-up Actions
- Ensure the student accepts the invitation and the account is created.
- HPC Admins to confirm access has been granted to Fritz.

## Related Parties
- HPC Admins
- 2nd Level Support team
- Software and Tools developers (for account-related queries)
- Training and Support Group Leader (for guidance on account types)
---

### 2023033142003062_Access%20to%20Alex%20cluster%20-%20iwal055h.md
# Ticket 2023033142003062

 # HPC Support Ticket: Access to Alex Cluster

## Keywords
- Access Request
- Alex Cluster
- Account Enablement

## Summary
- **User Request**: Access to Alex cluster.
- **Root Cause**: User account not enabled on the cluster.
- **Solution**: HPC Admin enabled the user's account on Alex cluster.

## Conversation Flow
1. **User**: Requests access to Alex cluster, provides user ID.
2. **HPC Admin**: Confirms account enablement on Alex cluster.
3. **User**: Acknowledges the enablement.

## Key Learnings
- Users may need access to specific clusters and can request it via support tickets.
- HPC Admins can enable user accounts on requested clusters.
- Common issue: User account not enabled on a specific cluster.
- Solution: Enable the user account on the requested cluster.

## Related Parties
- **HPC Admins**: Handle account enablement on clusters.
- **2nd Level Support Team**: May assist in escalated or complex access issues.
- **Head of Datacenter** and **Training and Support Group Leader**: Oversee overall operations and support.

## Additional Notes
- Always confirm the user ID and the specific cluster requested.
- Ensure proper communication of the account enablement to the user.
---

### 2024041942006436_Access%20Issue%20on%20Fritz.md
# Ticket 2024041942006436

 # HPC Support Ticket: Access Issue on Fritz

## Keywords
- Account activation
- Login issue
- Fritz access
- Account ID (b159cb15)
- csnhr.nhr.fau.de

## Summary
A user reported being unable to access Fritz despite successfully logging into the HPC system at csnhr.nhr.fau.de after accepting an account activation invitation.

## Root Cause
The user's account was activated, but access to Fritz was not properly configured or granted.

## Solution
- Verify the user's account permissions and ensure they have the necessary access rights to Fritz.
- Check if there are any additional steps required for Fritz access that were not completed during the initial account activation.
- Provide the user with instructions on how to request or enable Fritz access if it is a separate process.

## General Learnings
- Account activation does not always grant access to all HPC resources automatically.
- Specific access permissions may need to be configured separately for different HPC systems or resources.
- Users should be informed about any additional steps required to gain access to specific HPC resources after account activation.

## Actions Taken
- HPC Admins and 2nd Level Support team should review the user's account settings and permissions.
- Provide clear documentation or instructions to users on how to request access to specific HPC resources.
- Ensure that the account activation process includes information about additional access requirements if applicable.
---

### 2024061142003673_Rechte%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20Account%20%27barz104h%27%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20Projekt%20%27Tier3-Grundversor.md
# Ticket 2024061142003673

 # HPC Support Ticket Analysis

## Subject
Rechte für Account 'barz104h' für Projekt 'Tier3-Grundversorgung Universität Bamberg'

## Keywords
- Account Management
- Extended Rights
- Project Management
- HPC Portal
- Account Verlängerung
- Neue Accounts Einladen

## Root Cause
- User requested extended rights to manage existing and new accounts for a specific project.

## Conversation Summary
- **User Request**: Requested extended rights to manage accounts for the project.
- **HPC Admin Discussion**:
  - Confirmation needed if the request is formally sufficient.
  - Decision to grant rights based on the request coming from IT services.
- **HPC Admin Action**: Granted manager rights to the user.
- **User Confirmation**: Confirmed that the action was as intended.

## Solution
- **HPC Admin**: Granted manager rights to the user in the HPC portal, allowing them to extend existing accounts and invite new ones.

## General Learnings
- **Formal Request Confirmation**: Ensure that requests for extended rights are formally sufficient.
- **Portal Management**: Understand the process of granting manager rights in the HPC portal.
- **Communication**: Importance of clear communication between HPC admins and users to confirm actions.

## Documentation for Future Reference
- **Process**:
  1. Receive request for extended rights.
  2. Confirm the formal sufficiency of the request.
  3. Grant manager rights in the HPC portal.
  4. Confirm with the user that the action was successful.

- **Best Practices**:
  - Always confirm the formal sufficiency of requests for extended rights.
  - Ensure clear communication with the user to confirm actions taken.

## Conclusion
This ticket highlights the importance of formal request confirmation and clear communication in managing account rights within the HPC portal.
---

### 2024032642003856_Re%3A%20Your%20account%20%22ihpc112h%22%20for%20project%20%22Studentische%20Abschlu%C3%83%C2%9Farbei.md
# Ticket 2024032642003856

 # HPC Account Expiration and Extension

## Keywords
- HPC Account Expiration
- Account Extension
- Project Manager
- PI (Principal Investigator)
- HPC Support

## Problem
- User's HPC account is set to expire soon.
- User needs account extension to complete their Master's thesis.

## Root Cause
- The account has a predefined expiration date.
- The user's project (Master's thesis) extends beyond the account's expiration date.

## Solution
- User requested an extension from the HPC support team.
- HPC Admin extended the account until the end of the user's project duration (end of May).

## General Learnings
- Users should be aware of their account expiration dates.
- Extensions can be requested if the project duration allows it.
- Requests should be directed to the PI, project managers, or HPC support team.
- HPC Admins can assist with account extensions.

## Relevant Links
- [HPC Portal Documentation](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/)
- [HPC Support Email](mailto:hpc-support@fau.de)
- [NHR@FAU Website](https://hpc.fau.de/)
---

### 2024110742001262_Re%3A%20Your%20project%20%22EEA_TC%20-%20The%20Influence%20of%20Tropical%20Cyclones%20on%20Kilimanja.md
# Ticket 2024110742001262

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Summary

## Subject
Re: Your project "EEA_TC - The Influence of Tropical Cyclones on Kilimanjaro’s Glaciers (c104fa)" will soon expire at portal.hpc.fau.de

## Keywords
- Project extension
- Starter project
- Core-hours (c-h)
- Data storage
- Follow-up application
- Project quota
- User accounts

## What Can Be Learned
- **Project Extension Process**: Understanding the steps and requirements for extending a starter project.
- **Resource Allocation**: How to request additional resources such as data storage and core-hours.
- **Application Forms**: Difference between application forms for extensions and final reports.
- **User Account Management**: Responsibility for extending user accounts lies with the project's PI or delegated managers.

## Conversation Summary

### User Request
- **Project Extension**: User requested an extension for their starter project which is due to expire.
- **Resource Needs**: Project requires less than 1 million core-hours (around 700,000 c-h) but more active data storage (100 to 150 TB).

### HPC Admin Response
- **Extension Limit**: Starter project can only be extended by 2 months after running for 12 months.
- **Core-Hours**: Less than 1 million core-hours per year is not typical for an NHR project.
- **Data Storage**: Increased project quota on $WORK from 50 to 100 TB.
- **Application Form**: User should fill out the `NHR@FAU-short-application-form-JARDS.docx` for the extension.
- **Review Process**: Project can be extended while the review process is ongoing.
- **User Accounts**: Extending user accounts is the responsibility of the project's PI or delegated managers.

### Root Cause of the Problem
- **Resource Allocation**: User needed additional data storage and core-hours for their project.
- **Project Extension**: User needed guidance on the process for extending their starter project.

### Solution
- **Resource Allocation**: HPC Admin increased the project quota on $WORK from 50 to 100 TB.
- **Project Extension**: User was advised to fill out the `NHR@FAU-short-application-form-JARDS.docx` for the extension.
- **User Accounts**: User was informed that extending user accounts is their responsibility.

## Additional Notes
- **Austrian Science Fund**: Although not officially on NHR's whitelist, the project was handled like a DFG project.
- **Follow-Up**: User was advised to submit the application within two weeks and was informed about the extension process for user accounts.

This summary provides a concise overview of the conversation and the steps taken to address the user's requests and issues. It can serve as a reference for future support cases involving project extensions and resource allocation.
---

### 2023011142003264_project%20prolongation%20-%20p101ae%20-%20GPCRSCOMPEVO%20-%20Hildebrand_Leipzig.md
# Ticket 2023011142003264

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Summary

## Key Points Learned

1. **Project Extension Process**:
   - Users need to submit a new application form for project extensions.
   - The intermediate report should not exceed one page and should include any publications or manuscripts based on NHR@FAU results.
   - Administrative data in Part A sections 1 and 2 is mandatory.
   - Detailed explanation of requested resources is required in section 3.1.
   - Scientific Part B should describe the new project, justifying the newly applied HPC resources in section 4.8.

2. **Account Management**:
   - User accounts within a project are not automatically extended.
   - Project managers can extend accounts in the HPC Portal.
   - Accounts can be set to 'active' and a new date can be added, with the latest possible date being the last day of the project.

3. **Data Access**:
   - Data is retained for a grace period after project expiration.
   - Users can lose access to their data if the project expires and accounts are not extended.

4. **Project Prolongation**:
   - Projects can be extended by submitting a formal project prolongation.
   - The next cut-off date for large-scale projects is in early October.
   - Projects can be extended for up to 3 years, with a maximum of 30M core-h for 3 years.

5. **Application Submission**:
   - All project applications must be submitted via the JARDS portal.
   - Submission via email is no longer possible.

6. **Common Issues**:
   - Users may encounter issues with account status toggling between active and inactive due to incorrect date settings.
   - Users may lose access to data if the project expires and accounts are not extended.

## Root Causes and Solutions

1. **Account Status Toggling**:
   - **Root Cause**: Incorrect date setting (e.g., setting the new date to 2023 instead of 2024).
   - **Solution**: Ensure the correct date is set when extending account validity.

2. **Loss of Data Access**:
   - **Root Cause**: Project expiration and account not extended.
   - **Solution**: Extend the project and user accounts in the HPC Portal before expiration.

3. **Project Extension**:
   - **Root Cause**: Unawareness of the extension process.
   - **Solution**: Submit a new application form for project extensions and follow the guidelines provided by HPC Admins.

## Documentation for Support Employees

### Project Extension Process

1. **Submit a New Application Form**:
   - Download the up-to-date application form from [NHR@FAU-application-form](https://hpc.fau.de/files/2022/12/NHR@FAU-application-form.docx).
   - Fill in the administrative data in Part A sections 1 and 2.
   - Provide a detailed explanation of requested resources in section 3.1.
   - Describe the new project in Scientific Part B, justifying the newly applied HPC resources in section 4.8.

2. **Intermediate Report**:
   - The intermediate report should not exceed one page.
   - Include any publications or manuscripts based on NHR@FAU results.

### Account Management

1. **Extend Accounts**:
   - Log in to the HPC Portal.
   - Navigate to Manager -> Projekte -> $PROJEKT -> Accounts -> Archivierte Accounts -> $ACCOUNT -> Account bearbeiten.
   - Set the status to 'active' and add a new date. The latest possible date is the last day of the project.

### Data Access

1. **Grace Period**:
   - Data is retained for a grace period after project expiration.
   - Ensure accounts are extended to maintain access to data.

### Project Prolongation

1. **Submit a Formal Project Prolongation**:
   - Submit the prolongation proposal via the JARDS portal ([JARDS](https://jards.nhr-verein.de/)).
   - Follow the guidelines provided by HPC Admins for large-scale projects.

### Common Issues and Solutions

1. **Account Status Toggling**:
   - Ensure the correct date is set when extending account validity.

2. **Loss of Data Access**:
   - Extend the project and user accounts in the HPC Portal before expiration.

3. **Project Extension**:
   - Submit a new application form for project extensions and follow the guidelines provided by HPC Admins.

By following these guidelines, support employees can effectively manage project extensions, account management, data access, and common issues related to HPC projects.
---

### 2024121842001954_Re%3A%20Your%20account%20%22b193dc11%22%20for%20project%20%22Odeuropa%20Image%20Processing%20%28b193.md
# Ticket 2024121842001954

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- Account Extension
- Data Transfer
- New HPC Project Application
- Project Runtime Extension

## Summary
A user requested a one-month extension for their HPC account to transfer data exceeding 1 TB. The user also sought guidance on applying for a new HPC project as they were joining a new lab.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- The user needed additional time to transfer a large amount of data (over 1 TB).
- The user's supervisory team was unable to grant an extension due to project runtime limitations.

## Solution
- **Account Extension**: The HPC Admin granted a one-month extension for data transfer until the end of January 2025.
- **New HPC Project Application**: The user was advised to contact their new supervisors for guidance on applying for a new HPC project.
- **Project Runtime Extension**: The HPC Admin informed the user that the PI or project manager could apply for a three-month project runtime extension by sending a short explanation to `hpc-support@fau.de`.

## General Learnings
- Users may need extensions for data transfer due to large data volumes.
- Supervisors should be consulted for new HPC project applications.
- Project runtime extensions can be requested by the PI or project manager.

## Documentation Link
For further information, refer to the [HPC Portal Documentation](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/).

## Contact Information
- **HPC Support Email**: `hpc-support@fau.de`
- **HPC Website**: [https://hpc.fau.de/](https://hpc.fau.de/)

## Roles
- **HPC Admins**: Manage account extensions and provide guidance on project applications.
- **2nd Level Support Team**: Assist with technical issues and support.
- **Head of the Datacenter**: Oversee datacenter operations.
- **Training and Support Group Leader**: Lead training and support initiatives.
- **NHR Rechenzeit Support and Applications for Grants**: Handle runtime extensions and grant applications.
- **Software and Tools Developer**: Develop and maintain software tools for HPC.
---
