### 2023110742002576_Project%20folder%20not%20visible%20for%20new%20participants.md
# Ticket 2023110742002576

 # HPC-Support Ticket: Project Folder Not Visible for New Participants

## Keywords
- Project folder access
- New participants
- Fritz cluster
- /home/atuin/
- Access permissions

## Problem Description
New students participating in a large-scale computing project on the Fritz cluster cannot access their project folders under `/home/atuin/`, despite the folders existing. They are unable to see or access the main project folder and their individual subfolders.

## Root Cause
The root cause of the problem is likely related to access permissions for the new participants.

## Solution
- **Check Permissions**: Ensure that the new participants (b146dc13 and b146dc14) have the necessary permissions to access the project folder `/home/atuin/b146dc` and their individual subfolders.
- **Update Group Membership**: Verify that the new participants are added to the appropriate group that has access to the project folder.
- **Propagate Permissions**: If necessary, propagate the permissions to the subfolders to ensure consistent access.

## Actions Taken
- HPC Admins and 2nd Level Support team should review the access permissions and group memberships for the new participants.
- Adjust permissions and group memberships as needed to grant the required access.

## General Learning
- Always ensure new participants are added to the correct groups and have the necessary permissions to access project folders.
- Regularly review and update access permissions to maintain security and functionality.

## Follow-up
- Confirm with the users that they can now access their project folders.
- Document any changes made to permissions or group memberships for future reference.
---

### 2020080342001407_Antrag%20HPC-Berechtigung.md
# Ticket 2020080342001407

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Antrag HPC-Berechtigung

## Summary
- **Subject:** Antrag HPC-Berechtigung
- **User Issue:** Certificate has expired
- **User Request:** Application for HPC authorization

## Keywords
- HPC-Berechtigung
- Certificate expired
- HPC-Kennung
- Password setup
- HPC-Cafe
- HPC-Server
- Dienstleistungen
- RRZE
- FAU

## Conversation Details
- **User:** Submitted an application for HPC authorization.
- **HPC Admin:**
  - Initially deferred the request due to it being an HPC application.
  - Created an HPC identifier (`iwia024h`) for the user.
  - Instructed the user to set a password for the new identifier via `www.idm.fau.de`.
  - Noted that it may take a few hours for password changes to be recognized on HPC systems.
  - Mentioned that initial service setup on HPC servers may take up to 2 days.
  - Recommended attending the HPC-Cafe for new HPC users.
  - Provided details about the upcoming HPC-Cafe, which will be an online session.

## Root Cause
- The user's certificate had expired, necessitating a new HPC authorization application.

## Solution
- The HPC Admin created a new HPC identifier for the user.
- The user was instructed to set a password for the new identifier and wait for the services to be initialized on the HPC servers.

## General Learnings
- **HPC Authorization Process:**
  - Users need to submit an application for HPC authorization.
  - HPC Admins create a new identifier for the user.
  - Users must set a password for the new identifier via the provided link.
  - Initial setup on HPC servers may take up to 2 days.
- **HPC-Cafe:**
  - A resource for new HPC users, held every second Tuesday of the month.
  - Occasionally replaced by online sessions.

## Additional Notes
- **HPC Admin Actions:**
  - Deferred the request initially due to it being an HPC application.
  - Provided detailed instructions for password setup and service initialization.
  - Recommended attending the HPC-Cafe for additional support.
```
---

### 2023082442001247_HPC%20Angelegt.md
# Ticket 2023082442001247

 # HPC Support Ticket: HPC Angelegt

## Keywords
- HPC Antrag
- HPC-Kennung
- IdM-Kennung
- HPC-Café
- Online-Einführung
- Dokumentation
- Passwortänderungen
- HPC-Systeme
- HPC-Server
- Sponsor Cluster2024
- STAR-CCM+
- Ansys
- OpenFOAM
- neko
- eigene Software
- 100 Knoten

## Summary
- **HPC-Kennung**: iwst086h
- **IdM-Kennung**: me85kyse
- **Services**: STAR-CCM+, Ansys, OpenFOAM, neko, eigene Software
- **Nodes**: 100

## Steps and Information
1. **HPC-Kennung**: The HPC ID iwst086h has been created.
2. **IdM-Kennung**: The user should log in with their IdM ID me85kyse at [www.idm.fau.de](http://www.idm.fau.de) and set a password for the new ID under Services.
3. **Password Changes**: It generally takes a few hours until password changes are known on the HPC systems.
4. **Initial Setup**: It can take up to 2 days until the services are initially set up on the HPC servers.
5. **HPC-Café**: Recommended for new users, held every second Tuesday of the month at 4 p.m. in room 2.049, RRZE, Martensstr.1. The next HPC-Café will be a hybrid event. Details are on the [HPC-Café website](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/support/hpc-cafe/).
6. **Online Introduction**: For new users, held on the following Wednesday. Details are on the [HPC-Café website](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/support/hpc-cafe/).
7. **Documentation**: Available at [HPC Documentation](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/systems-documentation-instructions/getting-started/).

## Root Cause and Solution
- **Root Cause**: New user setup and initial configuration.
- **Solution**: Follow the provided instructions for setting up the HPC ID, attending the HPC-Café, and referring to the documentation for further assistance.

## Additional Notes
- **Sponsor**: Cluster2024
- **Software**: STAR-CCM+, Ansys, OpenFOAM, neko, eigene Software
- **Nodes**: 100

This report provides a summary of the steps and information required for new HPC users to set up their accounts and access necessary resources.
---

### 2024121842000482_expiration%20project%20SelBlockInv%20-%20Selected%20Block%20Inversion%20%28j101df%29.md
# Ticket 2024121842000482

 # HPC Support Ticket: Project Expiration and Data Retention

## Keywords
- Project expiration
- Data retention
- Account extension
- Follow-up application
- Technical approval
- Scientific review

## Summary
A user inquired about the expiration of their project and the need to copy relevant data from their accounts. The HPC admin provided information about the project's extended duration and the status of their follow-up application.

## Root Cause
- User received a reminder about the project expiration and was unsure about the data retention policy.
- User was unaware of the project's extended duration and the need to extend individual accounts.

## Solution
- **Project Duration**: The project (j101df) is extended until the end of March 2025.
- **Account Extension**: Users need to extend their individual accounts themselves, as the HPC admin cannot determine who is actively working on the project.
- **Follow-up Application**: The follow-up application has been technically approved and is currently under scientific review.

## General Learnings
- Always check the HPC portal for the most up-to-date information on project durations.
- Individual accounts need to be extended by the users themselves.
- Follow-up applications may be in different stages of approval, and users should be informed about the current status.

## Actions Taken
- HPC admin informed the user about the extended project duration.
- User was advised to extend their individual accounts.
- Status of the follow-up application was communicated to the user.

## Follow-up
- No further action required from the HPC admin.
- User will extend their accounts as needed.
---

### 2022102842000176_HPC-Account%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20LV%20ArchSup%20und%20Rechnerarchitektur%20-%20Exportkontrolle%20und%2.md
# Ticket 2022102842000176

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation: Export Control and Proliferation

## Keywords
- HPC-Account
- Exportkontrolle (Export Control)
- Proliferation
- Dual-Use-Verordnung (Dual-Use Regulation)
- Datenschutzbeauftragter (Data Protection Officer)
- Geheimschutzbeauftragter (Confidentiality Officer)

## Summary
- **Issue**: Large number of HPC accounts requested for students, some from "critical" countries.
- **Concern**: Compliance with export control and proliferation regulations.
- **Action**: Reminder about Dual-Use Regulation and offer of support from the university's Data and Confidentiality Officer.

## Root Cause
- Potential non-compliance with export control regulations due to student accounts from critical countries.

## Solution
- Awareness of Dual-Use Regulation.
- Consultation with the university's Data and Confidentiality Officer for individual reviews if necessary.

## General Learning
- HPC systems may fall under Dual-Use Regulation.
- Accounts for users from certain countries may require additional scrutiny.
- University's Data and Confidentiality Officer can provide support for compliance issues.

## Next Steps for Similar Issues
- Inform users about export control regulations.
- Direct users to the appropriate university contact for further assistance.
- Document all communications and actions taken for compliance purposes.
---

### 2020022042000851_HPC%20Account%20abgelaufen.md
# Ticket 2020022042000851

 ```markdown
# HPC Account Expiration Issue

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Account Expiration
- Vertrag (Contract)
- IdM-Übersicht (IdM Overview)
- ZUV (Central User Administration)

## Problem Description
- User's HPC account was locked as per the IdM overview.
- The user's account was linked to an expired contract.
- The user was unaware of the reason for the lock and sought assistance to reactivate the account.

## Root Cause
- The user's HPC account was tied to an "Employee" contract that was marked as expired by the ZUV.

## Solution
- The HPC Admin re-linked the user's HPC account to a "Doctorate Candidate" contract.
- The account was reactivated and the user was able to log in again.

## Lessons Learned
- HPC accounts are tied to specific contracts.
- Expiration of the associated contract can lead to the account being locked.
- Re-linking the account to a valid contract can resolve the issue.
- Communication with the ZUV is crucial for understanding contract statuses.
```
---

### 2021021942001888_Access%20to%20gwgk%20and%20gwgi%20accounts%20in%20meggie.md
# Ticket 2021021942001888

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Access to Multiple Group Accounts

## Keywords
- HPC Support
- Group Membership
- Account Access
- File Permissions
- Primary Group

## Summary
A user requested access to an additional group to facilitate collaboration between different working groups within the same institute.

## Root Cause
The user needed access to the `gwgi` group to allow members of another team to access their account if necessary.

## Solution
- The HPC Admin added the user's account (`gwgk005h`) to the `gwgi` group.
- Noted that the primary group remains `gwgk`, so any new files created will belong to the primary group by default.

## Lessons Learned
- Users can be added to multiple groups to facilitate collaboration.
- The primary group determines the default ownership of new files.
- Changes in group membership may take some time to propagate across all systems.

## Notes
- Ensure users are aware of the implications of primary group settings on file permissions.
- Communicate the potential delay in group membership changes taking effect.
```
---

### 2024030642001618_Adding%20other%20university%20collaborators.md
# Ticket 2024030642001618

 # Adding Collaborators from Other Universities to HPC Allocation

## Keywords
- Collaborators
- Tier 2 allocation
- HPC portal
- IdM account
- eduGAIN
- SSO account
- PI (Principal Investigator)
- FAU (Friedrich-Alexander-Universität Erlangen-Nürnberg)
- Utah State University

## Problem
- User wants to add collaborators from another university to their Tier 2 allocation on the HPC system.

## Root Cause
- Collaborators need access to the HPC portal and must be invited by the project's PI.

## Solution
1. **Collaborator Login Setup**:
   - Collaborators must be able to log into the HPC portal (`https://portal.hpc.fau.de/`).
   - They can use an IdM account (if they have one as guest researchers at FAU) or their SSO account from their home university via eduGAIN.
   - The home university's IT department may need to release required SSO attributes for the HPC portal.

2. **Invitation by PI**:
   - Once collaborators can log in, the PI of the project can invite them through the HPC portal.

## Notes
- The HPC support team cannot directly assist with SSO attribute release; this must be handled by the collaborator's home university IT department.
- The process involves two main steps: ensuring collaborators can log in and then having the PI invite them to the project.

## Related Roles
- HPC Admins: Provide guidance on the process.
- 2nd Level Support Team: May assist with technical issues related to the HPC portal.
- PI: Responsible for inviting collaborators to the project.
- Collaborators' Home University IT: Responsible for releasing SSO attributes if needed.
---

### 2024020142000728_dsam004h%20Zugang%20abgelaufen.md
# Ticket 2024020142000728

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Access Expiration

## Keywords
- Access expiration
- Account renewal
- Verlängerungsantrag
- HPC account
- Ablaufdatum
- Affiliation

## Problem
- User's access to the HPC cluster has expired.

## Root Cause
- The user's HPC account has reached its expiration date.

## Solution
- The user needs to fill out and submit a renewal application form.
- The form can be found at [HPC-Antrag](https://www.rrze.fau.de/files/2017/06/HPC-Antrag.pdf).
- The completed and signed form should be sent to the RRZE Servicetheke.
- The expiration date of the HPC account can also be linked to an affiliation (employee, doctoral student).

## General Learning
- HPC accounts have expiration dates.
- Users need to submit a renewal application to extend their access.
- The renewal process involves filling out a specific form and submitting it to the appropriate department.
- The expiration date can be tied to the user's affiliation status.
```
---

### 2023120642002718_HPC-Accountlaufzeit%20an%20Arbeitsvertrag%20koppeln.md
# Ticket 2023120642002718

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket: Coupling HPC Account Duration with Employment Contract

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Employment Contract
- Account Extension
- IDM (Identity Management)
- FAU (Friedrich-Alexander Universität Erlangen-Nürnberg)

## Problem
- User requested to couple the duration of an HPC account with the existence of an employment contract.
- The user is currently a master's student but will become a doctoral candidate and research assistant starting January 15.
- The employment contract does not yet exist, but the HPC account is set to expire at the end of the year.

## Solution
1. **Temporary Extension**: HPC Admin extended the account until January 31, 2024.
2. **IDM Update**: User was instructed to notify HPC Admin once the employment status is updated in the IDM system.
3. **Coupling with Contract**: HPC Admin confirmed that the HPC account is now linked to the employment contract.

## General Learnings
- HPC accounts can be temporarily extended while waiting for official updates in the IDM system.
- Coupling HPC account duration with employment contracts ensures continuous access for users transitioning between academic roles.
- Communication between users and HPC Admins is crucial for managing account extensions and updates.
```
---

### 2022050242000224_IdP-Freischaltung%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20portal.hpc.fau.de%20von%20NHR%40FAU%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20den%20Zu.md
# Ticket 2022050242000224

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Subject
IdP-Freischaltung für portal.hpc.fau.de von NHR@FAU für den Zugang zu Hochleistungsrechnern im Rahmen des Verbunds für Nationales Hochleistungsrechnen - RWTH

## Keywords
- IdP-Freischaltung
- portal.hpc.fau.de
- NHR@FAU
- Hochleistungsrechner
- DFN-AAI-Föderation
- SSO-Anmeldung
- Attribute
- Meta-Daten

## Root Cause
- The HPC Admin requested the activation of their service in the IdP to allow users from the institution to access the high-performance computers at NHR@FAU.

## Solution
- The IT ServiceDesk successfully completed the connection and activated the service in the IdP.

## What Can Be Learned
- **Process for IdP Activation**: Understanding the steps required to activate a service in the IdP, including the necessary attributes and metadata.
- **Communication**: Effective communication between the HPC Admin and the IT ServiceDesk to ensure the request is processed correctly.
- **SSO Integration**: The importance of Single Sign-On (SSO) for seamless access to HPC resources.
- **DFN-AAI-Föderation**: The role of the DFN-AAI-Föderation in managing access to academic resources.

## Additional Information
- **Attributes Required**:
  - 'urn:oid:2.5.4.42' - 'urn:mace:dir:attribute-def:givenName': 'givenName'
  - 'urn:oid:2.5.4.4' - 'urn:mace:dir:attribute-def:sn': 'sn'
  - 'urn:oid:0.9.2342.19200300.100.1.3' - 'urn:mace:dir:attribute-def:mail': 'mail'
  - 'urn:oid:1.3.6.1.4.1.5923.1.1.1.6' - 'urn:mace:dir:attribute-def:eduPersonPrincipalName': 'eduPersonPrincipalName'
  - 'urn:oid:1.3.6.1.4.1.5923.1.1.1.9' - 'urn:mace:dir:attribute-def:eduPersonScopedAffiliation': 'eduPersonScopedAffiliation'
- **Metadata Location**: [Metadata](https://portal.hpc.fau.de/saml/metadata)
- **Service Information**: [NHR Application Rules](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/systems-documentation-instructions/nhr-application-rules/)

## Conclusion
The successful activation of the service in the IdP demonstrates the importance of clear communication and understanding of the required attributes and metadata for SSO integration. This process ensures that users can access HPC resources seamlessly.
```
---

### 2025022742002828_Access%20to%20FAU%20cluster.md
# Ticket 2025022742002828

 # HPC Support Ticket: Access to FAU Cluster

## Keywords
- Access Request
- FAU Cluster
- Project Management
- SSO-Identity
- User Management

## Problem
- Users (students) requested access to the FAU cluster.
- Users provided their SSO-Identities.

## Root Cause
- The project manager for the relevant project has not been active on the system.
- Users' work may not be related to the existing project, requiring a new project to be created.

## Solution
- Users need to contact the project manager if their work is related to the existing project.
- If the work is not related, a new project needs to be created by the professor/head of the lecture chair.

## General Learnings
- User management within projects is handled by project managers.
- New projects need to be initiated by the professor/head of the lecture chair.
- HPC Admins can guide users to the appropriate project manager or professor for access requests.

## Next Steps
- Users should contact the project manager (Michael Krawez) if their work is related to the existing project.
- If not related, users should request their professor to create a new project.

## References
- HPC Admin: Thomas Gruber
- Project Manager: Michael Krawez
- Professor: Steffen Eger
- Users: Ran Zhang, Daniil
---

### 2024091642000831_Fwd%3A%20Your%20account%20%22iwi5191h%22%20for%20project%20%22Studentische%20Abschlu%C3%83%C2%9Farbe.md
# Ticket 2024091642000831

 # HPC Support Ticket: Account Extension Request

## Keywords
- Account Expiration
- Extension Request
- Project Manager
- PI (Principal Investigator)
- Master Thesis

## Summary
A user received an automated email notification that their HPC account is about to expire. The user requested an extension for their master thesis project.

## Root Cause
- The user's HPC account is set to expire in less than two weeks.
- The user needs an extension to continue working on their master thesis.

## Solution
- The user was instructed to contact the Principal Investigator (PI) or project managers to request an extension.
- PIs and project managers are responsible for account management within their projects.

## General Learnings
- Users should be aware of their account expiration dates.
- Extension requests should be directed to the PI or project managers.
- Automated notifications are sent to users when their accounts are about to expire.
- Users should provide relevant details and attachments when requesting extensions.

## Actions Taken
- The user was advised to contact the PI or project managers for an extension.
- No further action was required from the HPC support team at this stage.

## Follow-up
- The user should follow up with the PI or project managers to ensure the extension is processed.
- If there are any issues, the user can contact HPC support for further assistance.

## References
- [HPC Portal Documentation](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/)
- HPC Support Email: `hpc-support@fau.de`

### 2023052742000444_Reg%3A%20Incorrect%20password%20in%20HPC%20account.md
# Ticket 2023052742000444

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Incorrect Password Issue

## Keywords
- Password reset
- Permission denied
- HPC account
- IDM account
- Password synchronization delay

## Problem Description
The user was repeatedly getting a "permission denied" error despite changing the password in the IDM.

## Root Cause
- The user changed the password in the IDM but not in the HPC account.
- There is a delay in synchronizing the new password across all clusters.

## Solution
- Ensure the password is updated in the HPC account, not just the IDM.
- Wait several hours for the new password to be available on all clusters.

## Lessons Learned
- Always verify that the password change is applied to the correct account (HPC account in this case).
- Be aware of the delay in password synchronization across clusters.

## Ticket Status
- The issue was resolved after the user waited for the password synchronization to complete.
- The ticket was closed as the user confirmed that everything was working smoothly.
```
---

### 2020111342001794_HPC-Accounts%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20LV%20Architectures%20of%20Supercomputers.md
# Ticket 2020111342001794

 # HPC-Support Ticket: HPC Accounts for Architectures of Supercomputers Course

## Keywords
- HPC Accounts
- Architectures of Supercomputers
- Emmy-Cluster
- Meggie-Cluster
- Course Participants
- Account Expiration

## Summary
A professor requested HPC accounts for course participants on the Emmy and Meggie clusters. The accounts were needed for the duration of the course, with an expiration date one week after the course end.

## Root Cause
- The professor required HPC accounts for students enrolled in the Architectures of Supercomputers course.
- The accounts needed to be valid until one week after the course end date (19.2.2020).

## Solution
- HPC Admins created accounts with the prefix `hpcv` followed by a unique identifier for each student.
- The accounts were set to expire on 30.3.2020, ensuring they remain active until one week after the course end date.
- The accounts were associated with the Lehrstuhl für Informatik 3 and had the identifier `iwi3101`.

## General Learnings
- HPC accounts for course participants should be created with a clear expiration date to ensure they are active for the duration of the course.
- Communication between the professor and HPC Admins is crucial to ensure the correct accounts are created and configured.
- Accounts should be tracked and managed to ensure they are deactivated after the course ends to maintain system security and resource allocation.

## Actions Taken
- HPC Admins created the necessary accounts with the specified expiration date.
- The accounts were configured to provide access to the Emmy and Meggie clusters.
- The professor was informed about the account creation and expiration details.

## Follow-up
- Ensure that the accounts are deactivated after the specified expiration date.
- Monitor the usage of the accounts to ensure they are being used appropriately.
- Maintain clear communication with the professor for any future account requests or extensions.
---

### 2021021142000652_Bitte%20Aktivierungsschreiben%2C%20s.u.%20verschicken.md
# Ticket 2021021142000652

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC-Kennung
- IdM-Kennung
- Passwortänderungen
- HPC-Server
- HPC-Cafe
- Aktivierungsschreiben
- Tippfehler
- Standarddatum
- Testcluster
- sacctmgr

## General Learnings
- **Account Activation**: Users need to activate their IdM accounts and set passwords for new HPC accounts.
- **Delay in Service Setup**: It can take up to 2 days for initial services to be set up on HPC servers.
- **Password Propagation**: Password changes may take several hours to be recognized on HPC systems.
- **HPC-Cafe**: Recommended for new users, held every second Tuesday, currently online due to COVID-19.
- **Account Extension**: Accounts can be extended beyond the standard date upon request.
- **Ticket Management**: Tickets can be locked to specific users and moved between service desks.
- **User Group Management**: Users can be added to specific accounts and partitions using `sacctmgr`.

## Root Cause of Problems
- **Typo in User ID**: Incorrect user ID provided, corrected to `fy89gyno`.
- **Account Activation Delay**: Some users had not activated their IdM accounts, delaying HPC account setup.

## Solutions
- **Correct Typo**: Verify and correct user IDs as needed.
- **Wait for Activation**: Users need to activate their IdM accounts before HPC accounts can be fully set up.
- **Extend Account Duration**: Accounts can be extended by HPC Admins upon request.

## Specific Actions Taken
- **Accounts Created**: HPC accounts `hpc0002h` to `hpc0008h` were created for users.
- **Accounts Extended**: Accounts listed under #1 were extended to 30.11.21.
- **Users Added to Testcluster**: Users were added to the Testcluster with `sacctmgr`.

## Follow-up
- **Pending Activations**: Some users still need to activate their IdM accounts.
- **Ticket Locking**: Ticket was locked to a specific user after completion of tasks.
---

### 2021071442003162_prolongation%20of%20hpc%20account.md
# Ticket 2021071442003162

 # HPC Support Ticket: Prolongation of HPC Account

## Keywords
- HPC account prolongation
- IdM affiliation
- Gastwissenschaftler
- Beschäftigter (gewesen)
- PhD students
- VAULT storage

## Summary
A user is leaving their current position but needs to continue using HPC resources to wrap up work and publications. They inquire about the possibility and process of prolonging their HPC account.

## Root Cause
- User's HPC account is tied to their current affiliation, which is ending.
- User needs continued access to HPC resources for pending work.

## Solution
1. **IdM Affiliation Required**: An active IdM affiliation is necessary to use HPC resources.
2. **Application Form**: User needs to fill out the IdM application form available at [Antrag-IdMKennung-1.pdf](https://www.rrze.fau.de/files/2017/07/Antrag-IdMKennung-1.pdf).
3. **Affiliation Options**:
   - **Beschäftigter (gewesen)**: Possible for up to 9 months.
   - **Gastwissenschaftler**: Requires an additional letter from the chair.
4. **Mention HPC**: In the "Erläuterung" box on the application form.
5. **PhD Students**:
   - If planning to get their degree from FAU and registered in DocDaten/Graduiertenschule, they will have the status of Employees/Doctorate Candidates.
   - Otherwise, they should select "Gastwissenschaftler" and not "Gaststudierender".

## Additional Notes
- If the initial letter from the departing chair is refused, a letter from another professor may be required.
- The ServiceTheke may automatically prolong the HPC account upon receiving the new IdM affiliation. If not, HPC Services can do it on short notice.

## References
- [IdM Affiliations](https://www.idm.fau.de/dsms/docs/affiliations)
- [HPC Services FAU](http://hpc.fau.de/)

This documentation provides a guide for users and support employees regarding the prolongation of HPC accounts and the necessary IdM affiliations.
---

### 2024021442000785_Fehlender%20Zugriff%20auf%20Alex%20Cluster.md
# Ticket 2024021442000785

 # HPC-Support Ticket: Fehlender Zugriff auf Alex Cluster

## Keywords
- HPC-Ressourcen
- Zugriff
- Alex Cluster
- SSH-Login
- Tier3 Account
- Antrag

## Problem
- User hat Zugriff auf Woody und TinyGPU, aber nicht auf Alex Cluster.
- SSH-Login zu Alex ergibt die Meldung: "Dieses Konto ist momentan nicht verfügbar."

## Root Cause
- Freischaltung für Alex Cluster erfolgt für Tier3 Accounts nicht automatisch.
- Erhöhter Bedarf muss nachgewiesen werden, der nicht auf TinyGPU machbar ist.

## Solution
- User muss einen separaten Antrag für den Zugriff auf Alex Cluster stellen.
- Antrag finden unter: [Tier3 Access to Alex](https://hpc.fau.de/tier3-access-to-alex/)

## What to Learn
- Zugriff auf spezifische Cluster wie Alex erfordert möglicherweise zusätzliche Anträge.
- Tier3 Accounts benötigen einen Nachweis des erhöhten Bedarfs für den Zugriff auf bestimmte Ressourcen.
- HPC Admins können bei der Klärung von Zugriffsproblemen und der Bereitstellung von Antragsformularen unterstützen.
---

### 2022072142000051_Issue%20with%20the%20Access.md
# Ticket 2022072142000051

 # HPC Support Ticket: Issue with Access

## Keywords
- Access denied
- Password propagation
- HPC account
- Credentials

## Problem Description
- User unable to access HPC account despite using correct credentials.
- Access denied error message.

## Root Cause
- Recent password change not yet propagated to all HPC systems.

## Solution
- Wait for password propagation, which can take a few hours or until the next morning.
- Use the last password set before the change if the new one is not working.

## Ticket Conversation Summary
- **User**: Reported access denied issue despite using correct credentials.
- **HPC Admin**: Informed user about password propagation delay and suggested waiting.
- **User**: Confirmed issue persisted.
- **User**: Later confirmed access was restored.
- **HPC Admin**: Closed the ticket as resolved.

## General Learnings
- Password changes may not be immediate across all HPC systems.
- Users should wait for propagation or use the previous password if issues arise.
- Communication and patience are key in resolving such issues.
---

### 2018041142000735_HPC-Kennun.md
# Ticket 2018041142000735

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Subject: HPC-Kennung

### Keywords
- HPC-Kennung
- Self Service
- Dienstleistungen
- Passwort
- HPC-Server
- iwmv-Account
- Laufzeit
- FASTEST
- Meggie
- big-Queue
- Blockzeit-Projekt

### General Learnings
- **HPC-Kennung Creation**: A new HPC-Kennung (i.e., iwmv000h) is created for the user.
- **Self Service Password Setup**: Users need to set up their password via the Self Service portal under Datenübersicht/Dienstleistungen/Spezielle Dienstleistungen.
- **Service Delay**: It may take up to 2 days for the services to be fully set up on the HPC servers.
- **Account Transition**: The user's account is transitioned from one department to another (e.g., from LSTM to LFG).
- **Account Lifetime**: The new account has a fixed lifetime until the end of the year.
- **Resource Allocation**: The user is granted access to specific resources like FASTEST and Meggie for large-scale projects.

### Root Cause of the Problem
- The user required a new HPC-Kennung due to a change in their departmental affiliation.

### Solution
- A new iwmv-Account was created for the user.
- The user was instructed to set up their password via the Self Service portal.
- The user was granted access to specific HPC resources for their projects.

### Additional Notes
- The ticket was initially delayed due to the creation of a new HPC-Kennung.
- The user's access to resources like Meggie for big-Queue was discussed and approved for large-scale Blockzeit-Projekt.

This documentation can be used to assist future users who require similar account transitions or resource allocations.
---

### 2024040542002626_FAU%20CSNHR%20%26%20Fritz%20Zugang%3A%20Fehlende%20Standard%20Ordner.md
# Ticket 2024040542002626

 # HPC Support Ticket Analysis: FAU CSNHR & Fritz Zugang: Fehlende Standard Ordner

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Standard Ordner
- $HOME
- $WORK
- csnhr.nhr.fau.de
- fritz.nhr.fau.de
- ProxyJump
- Account Registration
- Automatisierte Registrierung

## Problem
- User cannot access `$HOME` and `$WORK` directories on `fritz.nhr.fau.de`.
- Connection to `fritz.nhr.fau.de` closes immediately with the message "This account is currently not available."
- User can log into `csnhr.nhr.fau.de` but encounters issues with directory creation.

## Root Cause
- The user's account was recently created, and the automated registration process for new accounts on the clusters is performed only once daily.
- The user accepted the invitation on the same day and attempted to access the clusters before the registration process was completed.

## Solution
- The user was informed that the account setup process takes until the next day to complete.
- The user was advised to try logging in again the next day.

## General Learnings
- New HPC accounts are usually available on the HPC systems one day after the invitation has been accepted.
- The HPC portal should display a message indicating the delay in account setup.
- Users should be aware of the automated registration process and the delay in account availability.
- Ensure that users understand the distinction between different clusters and their respective resources.

## Actions Taken
- The HPC Admin clarified the account setup process and advised the user to wait until the next day.
- The ticket was closed after the user acknowledged the information.

## Documentation Reference
- [HPC Portal Documentation](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/)

## Next Steps
- Monitor the user's account status to ensure successful registration.
- Provide additional support if the user encounters further issues after the registration process is complete.
---

### 2015112042001487_AW%3A%20HPC-Account%20hpc023%20am_at%20RRZE.md
# Ticket 2015112042001487

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Password Change
- IdM-System
- SSHA512 Hash
- Login Issues
- Password Policy

## Summary
- **Root Cause**: User's password did not meet the new password policy requirements after the system update.
- **Solution**: User was instructed to change the password via the IdM-Portal to ensure compatibility with the new SSHA512 hash algorithm.

## Detailed Analysis
- **Problem**: The user reported that the suggested passwords during the change process were rejected due to not meeting the new password policy requirements.
- **Action Taken**: The user was advised to log into the IdM-Portal and change the password for their HPC account to ensure it is hashed with the new SSHA512 algorithm.
- **Outcome**: The user followed the instructions but encountered issues with the password policy, which was noted as a minor bug.

## Lessons Learned
- **Password Policy Compliance**: Ensure that users are aware of the new password policy requirements to avoid login issues.
- **System Updates**: Regularly update users about system changes and their potential impact on account access.
- **User Support**: Provide clear instructions for password changes and be prepared to assist with any issues that arise.

## Next Steps
- **Documentation**: Update the user guide with detailed steps for changing passwords and complying with the new password policy.
- **Bug Report**: Address the issue of suggested passwords not meeting the policy requirements to improve user experience.

## References
- IdM-Portal: [https://www.idm.fau.de/](https://www.idm.fau.de/)
- HPC Support Email: support-hpc@fau.de

---

This report aims to assist HPC support employees in resolving similar issues related to password changes and system updates.
---

### 2021041942001786_Access%20to%20HUMAIN%20Department%20Cluster.md
# Ticket 2021041942001786

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket: Access to HUMAIN Department Cluster

## Keywords
- HPC Cluster Access
- Application Status
- Account Creation
- IdM Account
- HPC-Cafe

## Summary
A user applied for access to the HUMAIN Department Cluster but did not receive any updates on their application status for two months. The user's application forms were initially not correctly filled out and were lost in the process.

## Root Cause
- Incorrectly filled application forms.
- Miscommunication and delay in processing the application.

## Steps Taken
1. **User Inquiry**: The user inquired about the status of their application after not receiving any updates for two months.
2. **Admin Response**: The HPC Admin forwarded the request to the relevant coordinator to obtain the signed application forms.
3. **Coordinator Response**: The coordinator acknowledged that the application forms were not correctly filled out and provided the corrected forms.
4. **Account Creation**: The HPC Admin created an IdM account for the user and provided instructions for setting up the HPC credentials.
5. **User Correction**: The user pointed out a typo in their name in the response.
6. **Final Confirmation**: The HPC Admin confirmed the creation of the user's account and provided additional information about the HPC-Cafe.

## Solution
- Ensure application forms are correctly filled out and submitted.
- Regularly follow up on the status of the application if no updates are received.
- HPC Admins should double-check user details and ensure correct communication channels are used.

## General Learnings
- **Communication**: Clear and timely communication is crucial in handling user requests.
- **Form Accuracy**: Ensure all application forms are accurately filled out to avoid delays.
- **Follow-up**: Regular follow-ups can help in resolving issues promptly.
- **User Support**: Provide detailed instructions and support for new users to set up their accounts.
```
---

### 2025021742001295_Require%20access%20to%20HPC%20cluster%20-%20AG%20Bozec%20-%20Nikolaus-Fiebiger-Zentrum_UK-E.md
# Ticket 2025021742001295

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC access
- Project creation
- Tier3 clusters
- Application form
- Transcriptomics data
- PhD student
- Uniklinikum Erlangen
- Nikolaus-Fiebiger-Zentrum

## Summary
A PhD student requires access to the HPC cluster for handling large transcriptomics data. The student's research group is not yet a customer of the HPC facility, necessitating the creation of a new project.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- The research group is not yet registered as a customer with the HPC facility.
- The student needs guidance on the process for setting up a new project and gaining access to the HPC cluster.

## Solution
1. **Project Creation**: The student is advised to have the group leader (Professor) contact the HPC team to initiate the project creation process.
2. **Application Form**: The student is directed to fill out the application form for Tier3 clusters access.
3. **Documentation**: The HPC admin provides links to the documentation for project creation and additional steps required.

## Steps Taken
1. **Initial Request**: The student sends an email requesting access to the HPC cluster.
2. **Admin Response**: The HPC admin informs the student that the research group needs to set up a new project and provides a link to the documentation.
3. **Follow-up**: The student finds the application form for Tier3 clusters access and seeks confirmation on whether to fill it out.
4. **Admin Clarification**: The HPC admin confirms that the form should be filled out and provides updated documentation links.

## General Learnings
- New research groups need to set up a project before accessing the HPC cluster.
- The group leader (Professor) should initiate the project creation process.
- Documentation and application forms for Tier3 clusters access are available on the HPC website.
- Effective communication and follow-up are essential for resolving access issues.

## References
- [Request Creation of New Project](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/#request-creation-of-new-project)
- [Application for FAU Tier3 Access](https://hpc.fau.de/application-for-fau-tier3-access/)
- [FAU Tier3 Application](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/fau-tier3-application/)
---

### 2024082742001847_%5BJARDS_23088-2025_Q1%5D%20NHR-NormalDFG%20-%20b248cb%20-%20MultiTransBatt%20-%20H.%20Oberhofer_Uni.md
# Ticket 2024082742001847

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- Compute Time Application
- JARDS
- NHR@FAU
- Project ID
- Manager Rights
- HPC Portal
- SSH Keys
- ClusterCockpit
- Technical Review
- Scientific Review
- Project Advisor

## General Learnings
- **Application Process**: The compute time application process involves finalizing the application, signing it, and uploading it to JARDS or sending it via email.
- **Manager Rights**: Once the technical review starts, the PI receives manager rights to assign accounts and start using the HPC systems.
- **HPC Portal**: The HPC portal is used for managing invitations, SSH keys, and accessing job monitoring.
- **SSH Keys**: Access to HPC systems is exclusively via SSH keys, which must be uploaded by each user in the HPC portal.
- **Review Process**: The application undergoes both technical and scientific reviews. Positive reviews lead to project approval.
- **Project Advisor**: A project advisor is assigned to support the PI with any questions related to the project.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- The user needs to sign the application form and upload it to JARDS or send it via email for further processing.

## Solution
- The user should sign the application form either electronically or by printing, signing, and scanning it. Then, they should upload it to JARDS using the provided link or send it via email with the subject "Application for Compute Time No. 23088".
```
---

### 2023062742000095_Fritz%20Zugang.md
# Ticket 2023062742000095

 # HPC Support Ticket: Fritz Zugang

## Keywords
- Freischaltung
- NHR-Projekt
- HPC-Portal
- Exportkontrollerklärung
- Login
- PI-Login
- Slurm
- Accounts

## Summary
- **User Inquiry**: Status of access request for Fritz for the user and their student.
- **Root Cause**: Missing login and acceptance of export control declaration by the principal investigator (PI) in the HPC-Portal.
- **Solution**: PI needs to log in to the HPC-Portal and accept the export control declaration.

## Detailed Conversation
- **User**: Inquired about the status of the access request for Fritz.
- **HPC Admin**: Informed that the technical review for the NHR project is complete, but the PI's login and acceptance of the export control declaration are pending.
- **User**: Acknowledged the requirement and agreed to follow up with the PI.
- **HPC Admin**: Confirmed the accounts are now activated on Fritz and offered to either create new accounts via the HPC-Portal or reassign existing accounts to the NHR project.
- **User**: Opted to create new accounts for better oversight.

## Lessons Learned
- Access to NHR projects requires the PI to log in to the HPC-Portal and accept the export control declaration.
- Users can choose to create new accounts via the HPC-Portal or reassign existing accounts to the NHR project.
- Communication and follow-up with the PI are crucial for completing the access request process.

## References
- [HPC-Portal Usage Documentation](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/documentation-instructions/getting-started/nhrfau-hpc-portal-usage/)
---

### 2022072042001043_Verl%C3%83%C2%A4ngerung%20HPC-Nutzungsrechte_Binden%20an%20Besch%C3%83%C2%A4ftigung.md
# Ticket 2022072042001043

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Verlängerung
- Beschäftigung
- RRZE-Kontaktperson
- Dienstleistungspaket HPC
- HiWi-Stelle
- wissenschaftlicher Mitarbeiter
- Zugehörigkeit
- TRR154
- KP-Kennung
- RRZE-Servicetheke

## General Learnings
- Users may have multiple employment statuses, which can affect their HPC account.
- HPC accounts can be linked to specific employment statuses to avoid frequent renewal requests.
- The RRZE-Servicetheke handles certain account changes, such as those related to the KP-Kennung.
- Effective communication and quick response times are crucial for user satisfaction.

## Root Causes and Solutions

### Ticket 1: HPC Account Renewal
- **Root Cause**: User's HPC account was linked to a previous employment status that was about to expire.
- **Solution**: HPC Admin updated the user's HPC account to be linked to their current employment status as a wissenschaftlicher Mitarbeiter.

### Ticket 2: Multiple Employment Statuses
- **Root Cause**: User had two employment statuses, one of which was about to expire and was linked to their HPC account.
- **Solution**: HPC Admin updated the user's HPC account to be linked to the other, non-expiring employment status (TRR154).

### Ticket 3: KP-Kennung Update
- **Root Cause**: User requested their KP-Kennung to be updated to reflect their current role as a Kontaktperson.
- **Solution**: HPC Admin redirected the user to the RRZE-Servicetheke, as they handle KP-Kennung updates.

## Notes
- Users should be made aware of the different responsibilities of the HPC Admins and the RRZE-Servicetheke to ensure they contact the correct department for their needs.
- Regularly reviewing and updating user accounts can help prevent account expiration issues.
- Clear communication about the process for updating account information can help users navigate the system more effectively.
---

### 2023102642002676_HPC%20account%20activation.md
# Ticket 2023102642002676

 # HPC Account Activation Issue

## Keywords
- HPC account activation
- HPC portal
- IdM
- NoMachine Client
- Tier3 service
- Alex access
- TinyGPU

## Problem Description
- User received an email to set a password for a new HPC account.
- Account not visible in the HPC portal after the expected setup time.
- User can access HPC servers via NoMachine Client but cannot access "Alex".

## Root Cause
- Account created using the old PDF application form process, not managed via the HPC portal.
- User does not have automatic access to "Alex" as a Tier3 service user.

## Solution
- Set the password via IdM as the account was created in the old way.
- Submit the form at [Tier3 Access to Alex](https://hpc.fau.de/tier3-access-to-alex/) to request access to "Alex".

## General Learnings
- Accounts created via the old PDF application form are managed through IdM and not visible in the HPC portal.
- Tier3 service users have access to TinyGPU but need to request access to "Alex" separately.
- Users should be informed about the account management system and access rights for different services.
---

### 2024091342003048_Access-%20Probleme.md
# Ticket 2024091342003048

 # HPC Support Ticket: Access- Probleme

## Keywords
- Access issues
- Account availability

The user reported access issues with specific accounts (b169cb13 and b169cb12) when trying to access Fritz and Alex. The user could reach csnhr.nhr.fau.de but received the error message "This account is currently not available" when attempting to access Fritz or Alex.

### 42051780_Login-Probleme%20auf%20woody.md
# Ticket 42051780

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Login Problems on Woody

## Keywords
- Login issues
- Password change
- IDM
- Woody
- Frontends
- Clusters
- Nodes

## Problem Description
- User changed password via IDM but was unable to log in to Woody.

## Root Cause
- An error prevented the new password from being propagated to the HPC systems.

## Solution
- The error was resolved, allowing the new password to be recognized.
- The change was initially pushed to the Woody frontends and would take a few hours to propagate to all clusters and nodes.

## General Learnings
- Password changes via IDM may not immediately reflect on all HPC systems due to propagation delays.
- Resolving propagation errors can restore login functionality.
- Communicate the expected delay for full propagation to users.
```
---

### 2023042642004122_Archivierung%20eines%20abgelaufenen%20Accounts.md
# Ticket 2023042642004122

 # HPC Support Ticket: Archivierung eines abgelaufenen Accounts

## Keywords
- Account deactivation
- Data archiving
- File permissions
- Ownership transfer
- Data deletion

## Problem
- User attempted to archive data from a deactivated account (`nfcc09`) but encountered missing group read permissions on some files.

## Root Cause
- Insufficient file permissions prevented the user from accessing all necessary files for archiving.

## Solution
1. **Permission Verification**: HPC Admin requested confirmation from the original account owner to grant access.
2. **Permission Granting**: Upon receiving confirmation, HPC Admin set the necessary group read permissions on the relevant directories (`home/hpc/nfcc/nfcc09` and `home/vault/nfcc/nfcc09`).
3. **Data Archiving**: User was able to copy the relevant data.
4. **Data Deletion**: After confirming that the data was archived, the user requested the deletion of the directories. HPC Admin confirmed the deletion.

## General Learnings
- Always ensure proper file permissions are set for group access when archiving data from deactivated accounts.
- Obtain explicit permission from the original account owner before modifying file permissions or transferring ownership.
- Communicate clearly with the user and HPC Admin to ensure all necessary steps are taken for data archiving and deletion.

## Additional Notes
- The account `nfcc11` was also mentioned for deletion of a specific directory (`/home/titan/nfcc/nfcc11`), which the user did not have access to. HPC Admin confirmed the deletion of this directory as well.

## Conclusion
Proper communication and permission management are crucial for handling data from deactivated accounts. Ensure all parties are informed and permissions are correctly set to facilitate smooth data archiving and deletion processes.
---

### 2019061342002001_Clusterzugriff%20bccb15.md
# Ticket 2019061342002001

 # HPC Support Ticket: Cluster Access Extension

## Keywords
- Cluster access
- IDM account
- Account extension
- Postdoc
- University contract
- RRZE services
- HPC access

## Summary
A user inquired about extending their cluster access beyond their university contract end date. The user's IDM account indicated that their cluster access would remain active until a later date, but they wanted confirmation and information on any points to consider when their access ends.

## Root Cause
- User's university contract ending but needing continued access to the cluster for ongoing projects.

## Solution
- The HPC Admin advised the user to fill out the "Antrag auf IdM-Kennung" form, selecting "Beschäftigter (gewesen)" as the user type, and submit it to the RRZE-ServiceTheke. This process allows for the extension of RRZE services, including HPC access, for up to 9 months.
- The user confirmed they would follow the advised procedure.

## General Learnings
- Cluster access is tied to an active IDM account.
- Users can extend their IDM account and associated services by submitting the appropriate form.
- Without an active IDM account, HPC access is not possible.
- It is important to plan for account extensions if ongoing projects require continued access to HPC resources.

## Follow-up Actions
- Ensure the user submits the form and follows up on the extension process.
- Monitor the account status to confirm the extension is applied correctly.

## Relevant Links
- [Antrag auf IdM-Kennung Form](https://www.rrze.fau.de/files/2017/07/Antrag-IdMKennung-1.pdf)

## Support Team Involved
- HPC Admins
- 2nd Level Support Team (if needed for further assistance)

## Additional Notes
- The user's specific account (bccb15) and the dates mentioned are for reference and may vary for other users.
- Always refer users to the official forms and procedures for account extensions to ensure compliance with university policies.
---

### 2024021942000669_Vorbereitungen%20zur%20Umstellung%20des%20LS%20Statistik%20und%20%C3%83%C2%96konometrie%20%28wss2%29.md
# Ticket 2024021942000669

 ```markdown
# HPC-Support Ticket: Transition from Paper-Based HPC Applications to Online HPC Portal

## Keywords
- HPC Account Management
- Paper-Based HPC Applications
- Online HPC Portal
- SSH Keys
- Single Sign-On (SSO)
- Tier3-FAU-Grundversorgung
- Export Control Regulations
- ClusterCockpit
- Jupyterhub

## Summary
The HPC support team is transitioning HPC accounts from paper-based applications to an online HPC portal. This involves several steps, including confirming export control regulations, assigning manager rights, and categorizing HPC accounts.

## Problem
- An HPC account (wss272) has not been used since its creation in 2010.
- The account holder needs to decide whether to keep the account or let it expire.
- Transition steps for the HPC portal need to be communicated and followed.

## Solution
- The account holder decided to let the HPC account expire.
- The HPC admin set the expiration date in the IdM portal to 17.2.

## Steps for Transition to Online HPC Portal
1. **Principal Investigator (PI) Login and Confirmation**:
   - The PI must log in to the HPC portal and confirm export control regulations.
   - This step cannot be delegated and must be completed before further actions can be taken.

2. **Assigning Manager Rights**:
   - If the PI does not handle day-to-day HPC account management, they can designate one or more employees to receive manager rights in the HPC portal.
   - These employees must also log in to the HPC portal to have their rights assigned.

3. **Reviewing and Updating HPC Account List**:
   - The PI receives a list of active and recently expired HPC accounts.
   - The PI must review the list, update expiration dates, and categorize accounts into one of three categories:
     - Tier3 Grundversorgung (wss2100)
     - Studentische Abschlussarbeiten (wss2101)
     - Projektpartner (wss2102)

4. **Importing Accounts into HPC Portal**:
   - Once the list is reviewed and updated, the HPC accounts will be imported into the HPC portal.
   - Users will be able to see their accounts and upload SSH keys.

5. **Transition to SSH Key Access**:
   - After the import, access to HPC systems will be exclusively via SSH keys.
   - Passwords will no longer be stored for HPC accounts.

## Benefits of the HPC Portal
- Elimination of paper-based HPC applications.
- Increased security through the use of SSH keys.
- Self-management of account lifetimes and permissions by PIs and managers.
- Access to additional services like ClusterCockpit and Jupyterhub via Single Sign-On (SSO).

## Additional Information
- The HPC portal has been in use for NHR projects for nearly two years and has proven to be robust and functional.
- Detailed documentation and FAQs are available on the HPC website.

## Conclusion
The transition to the online HPC portal offers numerous advantages, including increased security, self-management capabilities, and access to additional services. The PI and designated managers play a crucial role in ensuring a smooth transition by following the outlined steps.
```
---

### 2025021142000994_iwb6%20datasets%20_home_janus_iwb6-datasets.md
# Ticket 2025021142000994

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: iwb6 datasets /home/janus/iwb6-datasets

## Summary
The directory `/home/janus/iwb6-datasets` had no owner. Previously, the owner was an expired account. A new owner was requested to prevent the directory from being deleted.

## Keywords
- Directory ownership
- Expired account
- Owner change
- Data management

## Problem
- The directory `/home/janus/iwb6-datasets` had no owner.
- The previous owner's account had expired.

## Solution
- A new owner was identified and approved by the relevant parties.
- The ownership of the directory was changed to the new owner's account.

## Steps Taken
1. **Identification of the Issue**: HPC Admin identified that the directory had no owner and the previous owner's account had expired.
2. **Request for New Owner**: HPC Admin requested a new owner from the relevant parties.
3. **Approval**: The new owner was approved by the relevant parties.
4. **Ownership Change**: HPC Admin changed the ownership of the directory to the new owner's account.

## Lessons Learned
- Regularly review directory ownership to prevent issues with expired accounts.
- Ensure there is a clear process for transferring ownership of directories when accounts expire.
- Communicate with relevant parties to ensure a smooth transition of ownership.
```
---

### 2021042042001907_HPC-Account%20Christian%20Schmitt.md
# Ticket 2021042042001907

 # HPC-Support Ticket: HPC-Account Request

## Keywords
- HPC Account Request
- Department Change
- Account Creation
- Group Assignment
- IdM-Kennung
- HPC-Cafe

## Summary
A user requested an HPC account for their final thesis work. The request was complicated by a recent department change, which affected the account creation process.

## Root Cause
- The user's department had recently changed to the Department of Data Science, causing issues with the account creation process.
- The main group for the user was not displayed, making it difficult to assign the correct HPC-Kennung.

## Actions Taken
- The HPC Admin consulted with another admin (TZ) to resolve the issue.
- The user was assigned the HPC-Kennung `mpwm030h` and instructed to set a password using their IdM-Kennung.
- The user was informed about the HPC-Cafe for additional support.

## Solution
- Use `mpwm101` as the Kundennummer (KuNu) and continue using `mpwmXXX` with the group `mpwm` for users from the Department of Data Science.
- Inform the user about the account creation process and provide resources for additional support, such as the HPC-Cafe.

## Follow-up
- The user should be able to log in and use the HPC system within 2 days.
- The user was encouraged to attend the HPC-Cafe for further assistance.

## Notes
- The department change caused some confusion and delay in the account creation process.
- The HPC-Cafe is a useful resource for new HPC users, providing support and guidance.
- Password changes may take several hours to take effect on the HPC systems.

## Related Tickets
- Ticket#2021042042002111

### 2024011842001037_Login%20to%20HPC%20Alex%20Cluster.md
# Ticket 2024011842001037

 # HPC Support Ticket: Login to HPC Alex Cluster

## Keywords
- HPC Login Issue
- Alex Cluster
- Tier3 Access
- Account Not Available
- HPC ID Creation
- Password Setup
- HPC Cafe
- Documentation

## Summary
A user encountered issues logging into the HPC Alex Cluster, receiving an error message stating that the account is currently not available. The user could log into `tinyx.nhr.fau.de` but not `alex.nhr.fau.de`.

## Root Cause
- The user's HPC account was not automatically enabled for Tier3 access to Alex.

## Solution
- To request Tier3 access to Alex, the user needs to work on a project with extended demands that are not feasible on TinyGPU but still below the NHR thresholds.
- The user must provide proof of extended demand, the efficiency of their jobs, and a short description of what they want to do on Alex.
- The user should follow the instructions provided in the link: [Tier3 Access to Alex](https://hpc.fau.de/tier3-access-to-alex/).

## Additional Information
- The user was informed about the HPC Cafe, which takes place every second Tuesday of the month at 4 p.m. in room 2.049, RRZE, Martensstr.1.
- An online introduction for new users is available on the following Wednesday.
- The user was directed to the HPC documentation for further assistance: [Getting Started](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/systems-documentation-instructions/getting-started/).

## Next Steps
- The user should follow the instructions to request Tier3 access to Alex.
- The user should attend the HPC Cafe and online introduction for new users for additional support.

## Closure
- The ticket was closed after providing the user with the necessary information to request Tier3 access to Alex.
---

### 2024042642002257_Re%3A%20%28extern%29%20Your%20account%20%22b116ba15%22%20for%20project%20%22HPC4AAI%20-%20Tier3-Proj.md
# Ticket 2024042642002257

 # HPC Support Ticket Conversation Analysis

## Keywords
- Account expiration
- Extension request
- Login issues
- Cookie problems
- Private mode
- Different browser

## General Learnings
- Users can request account extensions from their PI or project managers.
- Login issues can be caused by old cookies.
- Clearing cookies or using private mode/different browser can resolve login issues.

## Root Cause and Solution

### Account Expiration
- **Root Cause**: User's account was about to expire.
- **Solution**: User requested an extension until the end of August, which was granted.

### Login Issues
- **Root Cause**: Old cookies causing problems due to changes in the login mechanism.
- **Solution**:
  - Delete all cookies in the browser.
  - Delete specific cookies: `microsoftonline`, `hs-coburg.de`, `fau`.
  - Use private mode in the browser.
  - Use a different browser where the HPC portal was never visited.

## Support Actions
- HPC Admins granted the account extension.
- 2nd Level Support team provided guidance on resolving login issues.

## Documentation for Future Reference
- **Account Extension**: Users can request extensions through their PI or project managers.
- **Login Troubleshooting**:
  - Clear all cookies or specific cookies related to the HPC portal.
  - Use private mode or a different browser to avoid cookie-related issues.

This documentation can help support employees address similar issues in the future.
---

### 2022042942002406_Zugang%20zu%20HPC%20Ressourcen.md
# Ticket 2022042942002406

 # HPC Support Ticket: Access to HPC Resources

## Keywords

- The user needed access to specific HPC resources for a practical course.
- Accounts needed to be created via the IdM-Portal.

2. **Account Duration**: The user specified that the accounts should be active until the end of November.

- **Communication**: It is important to specify the duration for which the accounts are needed.
- **Timeline**: There may be a delay in the full availability of accounts and directories on the HPC systems.

- The user was informed about the potential delay in the full availability of the accounts and directories.

### 2022112942003124_account%20gwgi022h.md
# Ticket 2022112942003124

 # HPC Support Ticket: Account Group Transfer

## Keywords
- Account Transfer
- Group Membership
- File System Access
- IDM (Identity Management)
- Account Expiration

## Problem
- User requested transfer from one research group (gwgi) to another (gwgk) to gain access to the new group's Titan file system.

## Root Cause
- User's current account was associated with the wrong research group, limiting access to necessary file systems.

## Solution
- **Initial Consideration**: Move the user's account to the new group and transfer home/vault/work directories.
- **Final Decision**: Create a new account for the user in the gwgk group and set the old account to expire at the end of the year.

## Actions Taken
1. HPC Admins discussed the feasibility of transferring the account within the IDM system.
2. Decided against modifying the IDM due to complexity.
3. Created a new account for the user in the gwgk group.
4. Informed the user about the new account and the expiration of the old account.

## General Learnings
- Transferring accounts between groups may involve complex IDM changes.
- Creating a new account and setting the old one to expire can be a simpler solution.
- Communicate account changes and expiration dates clearly to the user.

## Related Topics
- Identity Management (IDM)
- Account Creation and Expiration
- File System Access and Permissions
---

 ```markdown
- The employment contract was not registered in the IDM system, making it impossible to link the account duration to the contract.

- Account reactivation after expiration may take several hours.
```

- User Login Requirement

### 2020042242001478_Anfrage%20f%C3%83%C2%BCr%20Rechenaccount%20zwecks%20Vorlesung.md
# Ticket 2020042242001478

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Summary

## Subject
Anfrage fÃ¼r Rechenaccount zwecks Vorlesung

## Keywords
- HPC Accounts
- Lehrveranstaltung
- Dichtefunktionaltheorie
- CIP-Pools
- Corona
- Woody
- IdM-Kennungen
- Initialpasswort
- IdM-Selfservice-Portal
- Vorlesungsaccounts
- Cluster-Zugang
- Prüfung

## What Can Be Learned
- **HPC Accounts for Lehrveranstaltungen**: HPC accounts can be created for educational purposes. Required information includes the name of the course and a complete list of IdM-Kennungen of the students.
- **Account Behavior**: The created accounts behave like normal HPC accounts.
- **Remote Work**: If CIP-Pools are Linux-based, remote work is possible.
- **Initial Passwords**: Initial passwords are not sent via email. Accounts are personalized and visible in the IdM-Selfservice-Portal, where passwords can be set.
- **Notification**: No notifications are sent to individual students for Vorlesungsaccounts. The instructor must inform students about their HPC-Kennung and how to set passwords.
- **Cluster Access**: Information about cluster access must be provided by the instructor.
- **Node Reservation**: If needed, a certain number of nodes can be reserved for defined times. This must be requested a few days in advance.
- **Account Duration**: Accounts can be set to run until the end of the semester. Changing the expiration date is possible but requires additional work.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- **CIP-Pools Closure**: Due to Corona, CIP-Pools were closed, necessitating alternative solutions for student projects.
- **Initial Password Confusion**: The user was unaware that initial passwords are not sent via email and must be set in the IdM-Selfservice-Portal.

## Solution
- **HPC Account Creation**: HPC accounts were created for the students with the necessary access.
- **Password Setting**: Instructors were informed to direct students to the IdM-Selfservice-Portal to set their passwords.
- **Account Duration**: Accounts were set to run until the end of the semester, ensuring they are available for the final exam.

## Additional Notes
- **Node Reservation**: The instructor decided not to reserve nodes as the projects will be done at the students' convenience.
- **Account Expiration**: The accounts will remain active until 30.09.2020, covering the exam period.

This summary provides a comprehensive overview of the support ticket conversation, highlighting key points and solutions for future reference.
---

### 2021022342003172_Regarding%20HPC%20access.md

 # HPC Support Ticket: Regarding HPC Access

## Keywords

- **HPC Access Application**: User needs to fill out the application form available at [HPC-Antrag](https://www.rrze.fau.de/files/2017/06/HPC-Antrag.pdf).
- **Guidance**: Detailed instructions for filling out the form can be found in the [Getting Started Guide](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/systems-documentation-instructions/getting-started/).
- **RRZE Customer ID**: This ID is associated with the chair, not the individual. User should ask their supervisor for this information.
- **IT Contact Person**: User should contact their supervisor to find out who the IT contact person is and how to reach them.
- **Signature Requirements**: The application form requires signatures from both the user and the IT contact person. Alternatively, the form can be signed by the user and forwarded via email by the IT contact person to confirm the request.

## General Learnings
- HPC accounts are linked to the chair, not the individual user.
- RRZE customer IDs are also associated with the chair.
- The application process requires coordination with the local IT contact person.
- Expired accounts typically necessitate a new application.

## Next Steps for Support
- Ensure the user has access to the application form and guidance documents.
- Verify that the user has contacted their supervisor for the RRZE customer ID and IT contact person information.
- Confirm receipt of the properly signed application form.
---

### 2023110242003146_Request%20for%20Extension%20of%20HPC%20Account%20Access.md
# Ticket 2023110242003146

 # HPC Support Ticket: Request for Extension of HPC Account Access

## Keywords
- HPC account extension
- Academic project
- Personal enrichment
- Expired account
- Research chair affiliation
- AIBE support

## Summary
A user requested an extension of their HPC account, which had expired, for personal enrichment and further exploration of an academic project.

## Root Cause
- The user's HPC account had expired.
- The user wanted to continue using the account for personal projects without an active affiliation to a research chair.

## Solution
- The HPC Admin informed the user that an extension is not possible without an affiliation to a research chair.
- The user was advised to seek support from their contact at AIBE.

## General Learnings
- HPC account extensions require an active affiliation with a research chair.
- Users should contact their respective research group or institution for support in such cases.
- Personal projects may not be sufficient grounds for account extensions without proper affiliation.

## Next Steps
- Users should ensure they have the necessary affiliations before requesting account extensions.
- HPC Admins should provide clear guidelines on account extension policies to users.
---

### 2024070442003489_AW%3A%20Your%20account%20%22dsam003h%22%20for%20project%20%22Projektpartner%20Tier3%20Grundversorgun.md
# Ticket 2024070442003489

 # HPC Account Extension Process

## Keywords
- HPC account extension
- Project manager
- PI (Principal Investigator)
- HPC portal
- Account expiration

## Problem
- User's HPC account is set to expire soon.
- User requires continued access to their HPC account.

## Root Cause
- The account has a predefined expiration date, and the user needs to extend it to continue their work.

## Solution
- The user should contact the project manager or PI to request an extension.
- The extension process is handled through the HPC portal by the PI or project manager.

## Steps to Follow
1. **User Notification**: The user receives an automated email notifying them of the upcoming account expiration.
2. **User Request**: The user contacts HPC support to inquire about extending their account.
3. **HPC Support Response**: HPC support advises the user to contact the project manager or PI for an extension.
4. **Extension Request**: The project manager or PI processes the extension request through the HPC portal.

## Additional Information
- **HPC Portal**: [HPC Portal](https://portal.hpc.fau.de/)
- **Documentation**: [HPC Portal Documentation](https://doc.nhr.fau.de/hpc-portal/)

## Roles Involved
- **HPC Admins**: Handle the technical aspects and provide support.
- **2nd Level Support Team**: Assist with user inquiries and troubleshooting.
- **Project Manager/PI**: Authorize and process account extensions.

## Conclusion
To extend an HPC account, the user must request an extension from the project manager or PI, who will then process the request through the HPC portal.
---

### 2020051142003119_Aenderung%20Gruppe%20von%20HPC-User.md
# Ticket 2020051142003119

 # HPC-Support Ticket: Changing User Group

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Group Change
- User Migration
- Data Transfer
- Account Expiration

## Summary
A user requests to change the group affiliation of a student's HPC account. The student currently belongs to one group but needs to be moved to another group while retaining access to specific resources.

## Root Cause
- User needs to change the group affiliation of an HPC account.
- Concerns about data transfer and account expiration.

## Conversation Details
- **User Request:**
  - Change group affiliation for a student's HPC account.
  - Ensure access to specific resources (e.g., Titan).
  - Query about data transfer (home and vault) during the migration process.

- **HPC Admin Response:**
  - New HPC credentials created for the user.
  - Notification about the new credentials and their availability.
  - Information about the expiration of the old account.

## Solution
- HPC Admin created a new HPC account for the user.
- The new account will be available on all HPC systems by the next morning.
- The user can set the password for the new account via the IdM portal.
- The old account will expire at the end of June.

## General Learnings
- Changing group affiliation may involve creating a new HPC account.
- Data transfer considerations should be addressed during the migration process.
- Users should be informed about the expiration of old accounts and the availability of new credentials.

## Next Steps for Support
- Ensure the new account is properly configured and accessible.
- Assist the user with any data transfer needs.
- Monitor the expiration of the old account and provide reminders if necessary.

---

This documentation can be used to assist with similar requests for changing group affiliations and managing HPC accounts.
---

### 2024082042000306_Daten%20aus%20Projekt%20%20b144dc.md
# Ticket 2024082042000306

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Data Retrieval After Project Expiration

## Keywords
- Data Retrieval
- Project Expiration
- Account Extension
- Data Storage Policy

## Problem
- User reported that data was still stored under an expired project account (b144dc15).
- Concern about data deletion after project expiration.

## Root Cause
- Project expiration led to concerns about data availability.

## Solution
- HPC Admin informed that data in $HOME, $WORK, $VAULT is stored for 3 months after account deactivation.
- Project and account were extended until the end of the month to allow data retrieval.
- User successfully copied the required data.

## General Learnings
- Data retention policy: Data in $HOME, $WORK, $VAULT is stored for 3 months after account deactivation.
- Temporary account extensions can be granted for data retrieval purposes.
- Users should be informed about data retention policies and procedures for accessing data after project expiration.
```
---

### 2025012442000186_HPC%20shared%20Verzeichnis%20iwi9-datasets.md

 ```markdown
# HPC Support Ticket: Shared Directory Reactivation

## Keywords
- HPC Portal
- Account Reactivation

- Verzeichnis Zuordnung

- A shared directory (`/home/janus/iwi9-datasets`) was not assigned to a valid user account.
- The directory owner's account was inactive, leading to potential deletion of the directory.

## Root Cause
- The owner of the shared directory had an inactive account in the HPC portal.

## Solution
- The user was requested to reactivate their account in the HPC portal to ensure the directory remains assigned to a valid user.
- The user confirmed the reactivation of their account.

- Regular account maintenance and reactivation are crucial for preserving shared resources.

- The user confirmed the reactivation.
- The ticket was closed after the user's confirmation.
```

### 2023092642002384_Sperrung%20des%20HPC-Zugriffs.md
# Ticket 2023092642002384

 # HPC-Support Ticket: Account Lockout Timing

## Keywords
- HPC Account
- Account Lockout
- Expiration Date
- Extension Request

## Summary
A user inquired about the exact date their HPC account would be locked and requested an extension.

## Root Cause
The user's HPC account was set to expire at the end of the semester, specifically on September 30.

## Conversation Details
- **User:** Asked about the exact lockout date (September 28 or 30) and requested a few days' extension.
- **HPC Admin:** Confirmed the account would be locked on September 30 and stated that an extension was not possible due to the expiration of the user's IDM identification on the same date.

## Solution
No extension was possible due to the linked expiration of the user's IDM identification.

## General Learning
- Account lockouts are tied to specific dates and cannot be extended if linked to other expiring credentials.
- Users should be aware of their account expiration dates and plan accordingly.

## Action Items for Support
- Confirm account expiration dates for users.
- Inform users about the impossibility of extensions if linked to other expiring credentials.
---

### 2024011842000351_Vorbereitungen%20zur%20Umstellung%20des%20FAPS%20von%20Papier-HPC-Antr%C3%83%C2%A4gen%20auf%20das%20.md
# Ticket 2024011842000351

 # HPC-Support Ticket Conversation Summary

## Subject
Vorbereitungen zur Umstellung des FAPS von Papier-HPC-Anträgen auf das online HPC-Portal

## Keywords
- HPC-Portal
- SSO-Login
- Manager-Rechte
- HPC-Kennungen
- Export-Kontrollbestimmungen
- SSH-Keys
- ClusterCockpit
- Jupyterhub

## General Learnings
- The HPC-Portal is being transitioned from paper-based HPC applications to an electronic portal.
- The Principal Investigator (PI) must log in to the HPC-Portal and accept the export control regulations.
- Manager rights can be assigned to designated employees for handling daily HPC tasks.
- HPC accounts need to be categorized and their expiration dates updated.
- The use of SSH keys increases security and will be mandatory for accessing HPC systems.
- The HPC-Portal provides various advantages, including the elimination of paper applications and self-management of account lifetimes.

## Root Cause of the Problem
- The PI had not yet logged into the HPC-Portal to accept the export control regulations, which was a prerequisite for further steps.

## Solution
- The PI logged into the HPC-Portal and accepted the export control regulations.
- Manager rights were assigned to designated employees to handle daily HPC tasks.
- The list of HPC accounts was edited, categorized, and their expiration dates updated.
- The HPC accounts were imported into the HPC-Portal, and users were informed about the transition.

## Additional Notes
- The HPC-Portal has been in use for NHR projects for almost two years and has proven to be robust and functional.
- Further features and statistics will be added to the HPC-Portal in the coming months.
- The use of SSH keys enhances security by requiring both a passphrase and the SSH key itself.
- ClusterCockpit and Jupyterhub will be accessible via Single Sign-on through the HPC-Portal.

## Conclusion
The transition to the HPC-Portal streamlines the management of HPC accounts and enhances security through the use of SSH keys. The PI and designated managers can now handle daily tasks more efficiently, and the portal provides additional features and statistics for better monitoring and management.
---

### 2022112542004041_missing%20HOME%20_home_hpc_b157be_b157be17%20for%20UID%20210354.md
# Ticket 2022112542004041

 # HPC Support Ticket: Missing Home Directory

## Keywords
- Missing home directory
- New account setup
- Login issues
- Directory creation delay

## Problem Description
- User received a new HPC account but the home directory was not created.
- User could log in to one system (meggie) without a directory but was kicked out of others (fritz and alex).

## Root Cause
- The home directory creation process was not completed automatically as expected.

## Solution
- **HPC Admin** informed the user that it usually takes until the day after accepting the invitation for all directories ($HOME, $WORK, $HPCVAULT) to be available.
- User was advised to wait and check back the next day.

## General Learnings
- New account setup may have delays in directory creation.
- Users should be informed about potential delays in the account setup process.
- Troubleshooting login issues may involve checking for missing directories.

## Related Documentation
- [NHR@FAU HPC Portal Usage](https://hpc.fau.de/systems-services/documentation-instructions/getting-started/nhrfau-hpc-portal-usage/)
